title: Vision-language extraction of pedon data (Module 2)
text: canonical case – “PDF (optionally) profile photo, give three classifications one-pager report” – chain everything single call. default provider local Gemma 4 edge: Photos go extract_munsell_from_photo(); site metadata extract_site_from_fieldsheet(). three follow validate--retry contract accept provider exposes $chat(prompt, ...). next vignette (v05_spatial_spectra_pipeline) shows SoilGrids spatial prior OSSL Vis-NIR predictions can fill remaining gaps pedon classification.
code: res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html"            # optional output report )  res$classifications$wrb$name #> "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  res$classifications$sibcs$name #> "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado"  res$classifications$usda$name #> "Rhodic Hapludox"

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: Take canonical Latossolo intentionally erase CEC base-saturation values lower horizons. fill back simulated OSSL predictions §3. classification incomplete pedon already differs: evidence grade reflects missing data – per-RSG trace records RSGs returned NA attributes erased.
code: pr_full   <- make_ferralsol_canonical() pr_partial <- pr_full$clone(deep = TRUE) pr_partial$horizons[3:5, c("cec_cmol", "bs_pct") := NA]  pr_partial$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, designation,                         clay_pct, cec_cmol, bs_pct, oc_pct)] #>    top_cm bottom_cm designation clay_pct cec_cmol bs_pct oc_pct #>     <num>     <num>      <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>  <num> #> 1:      0        15           A       50      8.0     24    2.0 #> 2:     15        35          AB       52      6.5     17    1.2 #> 3:     35        65          BA       55       NA     NA    0.6 #> 4:     65       130         Bw1       60       NA     NA    0.3 #> 5:    130       200         Bw2       60       NA     NA    0.2 res_partial <- classify_wrb2022(pr_partial, on_missing = "silent") res_partial$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Nitisols" res_partial$evidence_grade #> [1] "A"

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: prior probability vector RSGs source – regional SoilGrids extract, national Embrapa map, interpolated kriging surface, etc. vignette build inline example runs without network access: prior_consistency_check() confirms deterministic call (FR) supported prior: Now suppose deterministic key instead landed Cambisols. prior flag disagreement (Cambisols probability 0.12 vs dominant Ferralsols 0.62 – inconsistency margin): deterministic key never overwritten prior. check flags cases manual review warranted; user remains charge final assignment. Real production runs source prior spatial_prior_soilgrids() (live SoilGrids-WCS request) spatial_prior_embrapa():
code: # Synthetic prior consistent with the gneiss-Mata-Atlantica context: # Ferralsols dominate, with a tail of Acrisols and Cambisols. prior <- data.table::data.table(   rsg_code    = c("FR", "AC", "CM", "AL"),   probability = c(0.62, 0.20, 0.12, 0.06) ) prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 #> 4:       AL        0.06 chk <- prior_consistency_check(rsg_code = "FR", prior = prior, threshold = 0.05) chk #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $p #> [1] 0.62 #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $status #> [1] "consistent" #>  #> $note #> [1] "Assigned RSG 'FR' has prior probability 0.620 at this location (>= threshold 0.050)." #>  #> $top_prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 prior_consistency_check(rsg_code = "AL", prior = prior, threshold = 0.05) #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $p #> [1] 0.06 #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $status #> [1] "consistent" #>  #> $note #> [1] "Assigned RSG 'AL' has prior probability 0.060 at this location (>= threshold 0.050)." #>  #> $top_prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 prior <- spatial_prior_soilgrids(pr_partial, buffer_m = 250)

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: OSSL workflow : Pre-process raw spectra (SNV / Savitzky-Golay 1st derivative). predict_ossl_mbl() – memory-based learning (recommended); predict_ossl_plsr_local() – partial-least-squares local subset; predict_ossl_pretrained() – pre-trained Cubist RF model. Convert property’s prediction-interval width –D confidence grade via pi_to_confidence(). fill_from_spectra() writes predicted value horizons table adds provenance entry source = "predicted_spectra". production call look like (skipped vignette OSSL multi-GB dataset downloaded): vignette, simulate predicted values directly pedon$add_measurement(): predicted values now horizons table, provenance log records predicted_spectra:
code: fill_from_spectra(   pr_partial,   library     = "ossl",   region      = "south_america",   properties  = c("clay_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "oc_pct"),   method      = "mbl",   preprocess  = "snv+sg1",   k_neighbors = 100L,   ossl_library = "/path/to/ossl-soilsite-vnir.parquet" ) preds <- list(   list(idx = 3, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 5.5, confidence = 0.78),   list(idx = 3, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 14,  confidence = 0.72),   list(idx = 4, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 4.9, confidence = 0.79),   list(idx = 4, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 13,  confidence = 0.74),   list(idx = 5, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 4.7, confidence = 0.70),   list(idx = 5, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 13,  confidence = 0.71) )  pr_filled <- pr_partial$clone(deep = TRUE) for (p in preds) {   pr_filled$add_measurement(     horizon_idx = p$idx,     attribute   = p$attribute,     value       = p$value,     source      = "predicted_spectra",     confidence  = p$confidence,     overwrite   = TRUE   ) }  pr_filled$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, cec_cmol, bs_pct)] #>    top_cm bottom_cm cec_cmol bs_pct #>     <num>     <num>    <num>  <num> #> 1:      0        15      8.0     24 #> 2:     15        35      6.5     17 #> 3:     35        65      5.5     14 #> 4:     65       130      4.9     13 #> 5:    130       200      4.7     13 prov <- pr_filled$provenance prov[source == "predicted_spectra", .(horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence)] #>    horizon_idx attribute            source confidence #>          <int>    <char>            <char>      <num> #> 1:           3  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.78 #> 2:           3    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.72 #> 3:           4  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.79 #> 4:           4    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.74 #> 5:           5  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.70 #> 6:           5    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.71

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: OSSL fills missing CEC/BS, deterministic key complete dataset classification’s evidence grade reflects predicted source. evidence grade ladder profile across three workflows:
code: res_filled <- classify_wrb2022(pr_filled, on_missing = "silent") res_filled$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Ferralsols" res_filled$evidence_grade #> [1] "B" res_filled$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: combine_priors() merges multiple sources (SoilGrids + Embrapa + custom map) weights, returning single normalised prior can feed prior_consistency_check(): posterior_classify() take ClassificationResult prior, returning posterior probability RSGs (deterministic key contributes sharply peaked likelihood). Used ranking ambiguous fixtures active-learning loops.
code: combined <- combine_priors(   priors = list(     soilgrids = data.table::data.table(rsg_code = c("FR", "AC", "CM"),                                           probability = c(0.62, 0.20, 0.18)),     embrapa   = data.table::data.table(rsg_code = c("FR", "AC", "NT"),                                           probability = c(0.55, 0.30, 0.15))   ),   weights = c(soilgrids = 0.6, embrapa = 0.4) ) combined #>    rsg_code  probability #>      <char>        <num> #> 1:       FR 0.7139748011 #> 2:       AC 0.2841641547 #> 3:       CM 0.0017189621 #> 4:       NT 0.0001420821

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: deterministic key always runs first never overwritten. Module 3 gives probabilistic sanity check external maps warns disagreement. Module 4 fills missing horizon attributes spectra, full provenance, evidence grade tracks substitution. Putting together, can take partially-described pedon, fill gaps spectra, classify deterministically, cross-check result global map – one pipeline. next vignette (v06_wosis_benchmark) shows run whole stack scale WoSIS global pedon archive paper-grade validation.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: benchmark profile triple (P,T,C)(P, T, C): PP – PedonRecord assembled source dataset’s horizons + chemistry; TT – target RSG name source’s classification (e.g. WoSIS’ cwrb_reference_soil_group); CC – candidate classification produced classify_wrb2022(P). benchmark reports four numbers per source: Top-1 agreement – fraction profiles C$rsg=TC\$rsg = T. Top-3 agreement – fraction TT among three highest-likelihood RSGs trace (key trace + spatial prior, available). Coverage – fraction profiles CC definite RSG (“Regosols” catch-). Indeterminate-rate – fraction profiles C$rsgC\$rsg NA owing missing diagnostic attributes. Confusion matrices reported alongside headline numbers: RSGs soilKey confuses (e.g. Acrisol vs Lixisol differ base saturation, disagreement border informative).

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS

code: expected <- c(   HS = "Histosols", AT = "Anthrosols", TC = "Technosols", CR = "Cryosols",   LP = "Leptosols", SN = "Solonetz",   VR = "Vertisols", SC = "Solonchaks",   GL = "Gleysols",  AN = make_solonetz_canonical,   VR = make_vertisol_canonical,  SC = make_solonchak_canonical,   GL = make_gleysol_canonical,   AN = make_andosol_canonical,   PZ = make_podzol_canonical,    PT = make_plinthosol_canonical,   PL = make_planosol_canonical,  ST = make_stagnosol_canonical,   NT = make_nitisol_canonical,   FR = make_ferralsol_canonical,   CH = make_chernozem_canonical, KS = make_kastanozem_canonical,   PH = make_phaeozem_canonical,  UM = make_umbrisol_canonical,   DU = make_durisol_canonical,   GY = make_gypsisol_canonical,   CL = make_calcisol_canonical,  RT = make_arenosol_canonical,   FL = make_fluvisol_canonical )  bench <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(expected), function(code) {   fx <- fixfns[[code]]()   res <- classify_wrb2022(fx, on_missing = "silent")   data.frame(     fixture  = code,     target   = expected[[code]],     assigned = res$rsg_or_order,     name     = res$name,     grade    = res$evidence_grade,     match    = res$rsg_or_order == expected[[code]]   ) }))  knitr::kable(bench[, c("fixture", "target", "assigned", "match", "grade")]) data.frame(   metric = c("n_profiles", "top1_agreement", title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: Beyond RSG, classify_wrb2022() returns principal- supplementary-qualifier lists. benchmark can deepened track agreement qualifier prefix soil name (strongest principal fired). canonical fixtures: -specific principal qualifier per canonical fixture.
code: qualifier_prefix <- vapply(names(expected), function(code) {   fx <- fixfns[[code]]()   res <- classify_wrb2022(fx, on_missing = "silent")   if (length(res$qualifiers$principal) == 0) NA_character_   else res$qualifiers$principal[1] }, character(1))  knitr::kable(   data.frame(fixture          = names(qualifier_prefix),              principal_prefix = qualifier_prefix),   caption = "Most-specific principal qualifier per canonical fixture." )

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: mini-benchmark proves package’s internal consistency. paper-grade benchmark runs flow ISRIC WoSIS, global archive ~118 000 georeferenced pedon descriptions WRB 2014 / 2022 classifications. protocol intentionally separated package (live download vignettes) can re-run controlled date. driver script shipped inst/benchmarks/run_wosis_benchmark.R. Skeleton: flow extends naturally : WoSIS subsets region – e.g. South America , Brazilian profiles (relevant SiBCS cross-system check). WoSIS profiles missing one attributes – exercise gap-filling pipeline (vignette 05) report agreement uplift predicted_spectra. WoSIS profiles labelled v2014 vs v2022 RSGs – benchmark separates soilKey’s v2022-key penalised WoSIS used deprecated RSG name.
code: library(soilKey)  # 1. Pull a snapshot of WoSIS profiles via the WoSIS API. profiles <- read_wosis_profiles(   url     = "https://wosis.isric.org/api/v3/profiles?format=json",   page_size = 500L )  # 2. Build a PedonRecord per profile. pedons <- lapply(profiles, build_pedon_from_wosis)  # 3. Classify each through the v0.9.3 key. classifications <- lapply(pedons, classify_wrb2022, on_missing = "silent")  # 4. Compare against the WoSIS-recorded RSG. bench <- mapply(function(c, p) {   data.frame(     profile_id  = p$site$id,     target_rsg  = p$site$wosis_rsg,     assigned    = c$rsg_or_order,     grade       = c$evidence_grade,     match       = c$rsg_or_order == p$site$wosis_rsg   ) }, classifications, pedons, SIMPLIFY = FALSE) bench <- do.call(rbind, bench)  # 5. Headline numbers. list(   n              = nrow(bench),   top1           = mean(bench$match, na.rm = TRUE),   indeterminate  = mean(is.na(bench$assigned)),   pct_grade_A    = mean(bench$grade == "A"),   by_rsg         = table(bench$target_rsg, bench$assigned) )

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS

Confusion matrix (RSG-level) qualifier-prefix agreement. Per-RSG agreement common confused pair . Evidence-grade distribution. Notable disagreements diagnostic-level traces, QA. script used periodic regression run ships minor soilKey release.

code: # Field GPS coordinates of the planned profile pit. field_lat <- -22.7 field_lon <- -43.7

what: 2. Spatial guide – before any pedon data
title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: soil_classes_at_location() queries SoilGrids 2.0 (WRB-coded raster user provides) returns ranked list likely classes plus canonical attribute thresholds distinguish . function classify – tells pedologist “likely standing Ferralsol; look confirm”.
code: guide <- soil_classes_at_location(   lat        = field_lat,   lon        = field_lon,   system     = "wrb2022",   source_url = "https://files.isric.org/soilgrids/latest/data/wrb/MostProbable.vrt" )  guide$distribution #> # Ranked candidate classes: #> # rsg_code  rsg_name      probability #> # FR        Ferralsols    0.62 #> # AC        Acrisols      0.21 #> # NT        Nitisols      0.12 #> # CM        Cambisols     0.05 guide$typical_attributes #> # Per-class diagnostic thresholds to confirm in the field.

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: pedologist arrives pit, photographs wall Munsell chart, scans field sheet, exports survey report PDF. classify_from_documents() chains entire downstream pipeline – VLM extraction, three classifications, optional report rendering – single call. default provider local Gemma 4 edge (gemma4:e4b, ~3 GB, multimodal text + image + audio) via Ollama – API key, data leaving laptop. Pull model : Every extracted attribute stamped source = "extracted_vlm" PedonRecord’s provenance log; deterministic key consumed PedonRecord unaware value got . architectural invariant – key never delegated model – holds. rest vignette keep working populated pedon res$pedon.
code: ollama pull gemma4:e4b ollama serve res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html",   provider = "ollama"  # default; uses gemma4:e4b )  res$classifications$wrb$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" res$classifications$sibcs$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado" res$classifications$usda$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox" # For a runnable demo without Ollama / a real PDF, reuse the # canonical Ferralsol fixture -- the downstream code is the same. pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: Vis-NIR scan available surface horizon, classify_by_spectral_neighbours() adds another evidence layer. finds K spectrally similar OSSL profiles within regional radius returns probabilistic class prediction. biome-aware regional filter prevents analogy drifting non-tropical reference soils.
code: # Hypothetical: a real OSSL South-America library with WRB labels # obtained via `download_ossl_subset_with_labels()`. ossl_lib <- download_ossl_subset_with_labels(   region          = "south_america",   max_distance_km = 10 )  # Pull the surface-horizon Vis-NIR scan from the populated pedon. query_spectrum <- pedon$spectra$vnir[1, ]  spectral <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum     = query_spectrum,   ossl_library = ossl_lib,   k            = 25,   region       = list(lat = field_lat, lon = field_lon,                       radius_km = 500) ) spectral$distribution #> # class    n_neighbours  probability #> # FR              22       0.88 #> # AC               2       0.08 #> # NT               1       0.04 spectral$neighbours #> # The 25 closest OSSL profiles + their distances + labels.

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: canonical step. classify_wrb2022() / classify_sibcs() / classify_usda() walk canonical YAML rules populated PedonRecord.
code: cls_wrb   <- classify_wrb2022(pedon, on_missing = "silent") cls_sibcs <- classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE) cls_usda  <- classify_usda(pedon)  cls_wrb$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" cls_sibcs$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado" cls_usda$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"  # Each ClassificationResult carries the full key trace, the per- # attribute provenance, and an evidence grade A/B/C/D. cls_wrb$evidence_grade #> [1] "A" length(cls_wrb$trace)         # number of RSGs tested before assignment #> [1] 16

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: report() writes self-contained HTML one-pager cross-system summary, full key trace, evidence grade, qualifiers, ambiguities, missing-data hints, horizons table, per-source provenance summary. output single HTML file inline CSS – external network requests, suitable emailing colleague attaching laudo.
code: results <- list(wrb = cls_wrb, sibcs = cls_sibcs, usda = cls_usda) report(results, file = "perfil_042.html", pedon = pedon)

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: report_to_qgis() produces multi-layer GeoPackage (.gpkg) QGIS reads natively. GeoPackage carries three layers: pedon_point – POINT geometry profile coordinates classification metadata attributes (WRB / SiBCS / USDA names, RSG / Ordem / Order codes, evidence grades, principal qualifiers, supplementary qualifiers, hyperlink rendered HTML report). horizons_table – one row per horizon, canonical horizon-schema attributes. Joined pedon_point site_id. provenance_log – per-(horizon, attribute, source) provenance rows downstream auditing. QGIS: Layer → Add Layer → Add Vector Layer → perfil_042.gpkg. point appears canvas classification metadata feature pop-; styling rules can map symbol colour evidence grade assigned RSG.
code: results <- list(wrb = cls_wrb, sibcs = cls_sibcs, usda = cls_usda) report_to_qgis(   pedon           = pedon,   classifications = results,   file            = "perfil_042.gpkg",   report_html     = "perfil_042.html" )

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: step’s output carries explicit provenance next; final evidence_grade reflects worst-source rule applied attributes decisive assigned name. Two pedologists running pipeline documents get output bit--bit.
code: # Pipeline summary: # #   field GPS      ->  soil_classes_at_location()         "what to expect" #                                  | #                                  v #   PDF + photo    ->  classify_from_documents() (Gemma 4)  populates PedonRecord #                                  | #                                  v #   Vis-NIR scan   ->  classify_by_spectral_neighbours()    spectral prior #                                  | #                                  v #                  ->  classify_wrb2022()  + classify_sibcs() + classify_usda() #                                  |       (the deterministic step -- canonical) #                                  v #                  ->  report() / report_to_qgis()         deliverables

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: soilKey separates four distinct stages: Spatial guides (soil_classes_at_location) – expectations soil-class raster. Extraction (classify_from_documents, extract_*) – VLM populates PedonRecord, never classifies. Spectral analogy (classify_by_spectral_neighbours) – OSSL nearest-neighbour analogy prior. Deterministic classification (classify_wrb2022 / classify_sibcs / classify_usda) – canonical step. Plus two delivery formats: HTML reports (report) GeoPackage exports (report_to_qgis). four stages preserve provenance evidence grading; deterministic key remains thing assigns class.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Automated soil profile classification WRB 2022 (4th ed.), USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th ed.), Brazilian SiBCS (5ª edição). three systems wired end--end deepest categorical level. Multimodal extraction, spatial priors, OSSL spectroscopy explicit per-attribute provenance — without ever delegating taxonomic key language model.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: canonical Brazilian Latossolo Vermelho Distrocoeso Mata Atlântica gneiss, classified end--end across three canonical systems deepest level: WRB delivers complete Chapter 6 name — four principal qualifiers + five supplementary qualifiers canonical order. SiBCS descends four hierarchical levels (Ordem → Subordem → Grande Grupo → Subgrupo) plus 5th-level Família 15 orthogonal adjectival dimensions (Família label includes adjectives sufficient evidence; richer profiles produce longer labels). USDA Soil Taxonomy walks complete Path C (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup) per Keys Soil Taxonomy 13th ed. three keys deterministic R code driven versioned YAML rules.
code: library(soilKey)  pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()  # WRB 2022: full Chapter 6 name (RSG + qualifiers + specifiers) classify_wrb2022(pedon)$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  # SiBCS 5a ed.: 4o nivel (Subgrupo) + Familia (5o nivel) classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE)$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado"  # USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed: Order -> Suborder -> Great Group -> Subgroup classify_usda(pedon)$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: public, mantained, end--end implementation three major soil classification systems. WRB acknowledges (4th-edition preface) internal classification algorithms exist within IUSS Working Group released. U.S. SoilTaxonomy package CRAN provides lookup tables key. zero public software SiBCS. soilKey closes gap three principles: taxonomic key never delegated language model. LLMs restricted schema-validated extraction. Every classification deterministic walk versioned YAML rules full decision trace. Every value carries provenance tag. measured · predicted_spectra · extracted_vlm · inferred_prior · user_assumed. result’s evidence grade (–D) summarises log callers always know robust classification . Side modules never overrule key. Spatial priors flag inconsistencies silently change assigned RSG; spectral predictions fill missing attributes explicit confidence; multimodal extraction pulls structured data without writing class names.

what: 1. Build a PedonRecord from horizon data
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: library(soilKey)  pr <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(     id              = "BR-LV-001",     lat             = -22.5, lon = -43.7,     country         = "BR",     parent_material = "gneiss"   ),   horizons = data.frame(     top_cm    = c(0,    15,   35,   65,   130),     bottom_cm = c(15,   35,   65,   130,  200),     designation        = c("A",  "AB", "BA", "Bw1","Bw2"),     munsell_hue_moist  = rep("2.5YR", 5),     munsell_value_moist  = c(3, 3, 3, 4, 4),     munsell_chroma_moist = c(4, 4, 6, 6, 6),     clay_pct = c(50, 52, 55, 60, 60),     silt_pct = c(15, 14, 10,  8,  8),     sand_pct = c(35, 34, 35, 32, 32),     cec_cmol = c(8, 6.5, 5.5, 5.0, 4.8),     bs_pct   = c(24, 17, 14, 13, 13),     al_cmol  = c(0.7, 0.8, 0.6, 0.5, 0.5),     ph_h2o   = c(4.8, 4.7, 4.7, 4.8, 4.9),     ph_kcl   = c(4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.1, 4.2),     oc_pct   = c(2.0, 1.2, 0.6, 0.3, 0.2)   ) )

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification

code: # Vis-NIR spectrum per horizon, OSSL backbone: pr$spectra$vnir <- my_spectra_matrix  fill_from_spectra(   pr,   library    = "ossl",   region     = "south_america",   properties = c("clay_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "oc_pct"),   method     = "mbl" ) # Now classify_wrb2022(pr)$evidence_grade may be "B" (predicted_spectra) # instead of "A" (measured) — provenance survives.

what: WoSIS run (paper-grade baseline, real external data)
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: 100 South-America profiles pulled WoSIS GraphQL maximal attribute query (24 *Values fields per layer); classified classify_wrb2022(). Top-1 agreement stratified data-coverage tier: 0 / 5 Tier-1 classifier failure. forensic walkthrough every miss (inst/benchmarks/reports/wosis_forensic_2026-04-30.md) shows: 1 / 5: defensible disagreement different WRB edition (WoSIS labelled “Acrisol” using pre-2022 source; soilKey WRB 2022 says Ferralsol data CEC ≪ argic threshold). 1 / 5: indeterminate due missing exchangeable cations WoSIS — trace says missing: bs_pct. Package correctly returns indeterminate rather guessing. 3 / 5: indeterminate due systematic WoSIS schema gap — WoSIS slickensides field . soilKey assigns next--defensible RSG WRB Ch 4 chave order. WoSIS target informed field morphology database archive. Genuine classifier failures: 0 / 5. apparent 0 % top-1 data-availability statement WoSIS, correctness statement soilKey.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification

Optional region filter (lat / lon / radius_km) keeps analogy biome-aware: Cerrado profile shouldn’t analogised Boreal taiga. Distance computed PLS-score space resemble installed (matching OSSL reference workflow, Ramirez-Lopez et al. 2013), PCA fallback otherwise. guides, classifiers. populate priors deterministic key runs. architectural invariant — “key never delegated model” — still holds: canonical assignment still comes classify_wrb2022() / classify_sibcs() / classify_usda() consuming fully populated PedonRecord.
code: data(ossl_demo_sa) ossl_demo_sa$Yr$wrb_rsg <- c(...)  # real OSSL ships labels; demo lacks them  res <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum     = my_vnir_spectrum,   ossl_library = ossl_demo_sa,   k            = 25,   region       = list(lat = -22.7, lon = -43.7, radius_km = 500) ) res$distribution    # ranked classes with P(class | spectral neighbours) res$neighbours      # the 25 closest profiles + their distances + labels

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: soilKey separates extraction (multimodal LLM) classification (deterministic R code driven versioned YAML rules). VLM never classifies; every value extracts carries source = "extracted_vlm" deterministic key consumes PedonRecord unaware value got . default local stack uses Gemma 4 via Ollama (gemma4:e4b, ~3 GB, multimodal text+image+audio). Cloud providers (anthropic / openai / google) remain one argument away. full canonical pipeline – extract PDF + extract Munsell photo + classify three systems + render report – one function call: Switch model / provider one argument: MockVLMProvider (offline, schema-validated) documented v04_vlm_extraction.Rmd tests CI runs.
code: library(soilKey)  # One-liner. Local-first; no API key needed; data never leaves your machine. res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html"          # optional self-contained HTML output )  res$classifications$wrb$name #> "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  res$classifications$sibcs$name #> "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado"  res$classifications$usda$name #> "Rhodic Hapludox" classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "ollama",   model = "gemma4:31b") classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "anthropic")  # claude-sonnet-4-7 classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "openai")     # gpt-4o classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "google")     # gemini-2.0-pro

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Every value used key recorded pedon$provenance : attribute : column name source : measured / predicted_spectra / extracted_vlm / inferred_prior / user_assumed confidence : [0, 1] (NA measured) notes : free-form (often source quote) ClassificationResult$evidence_grade worst-source rule applied attributes actually decisive classification — Ferralsol classified entirely lab data yields ; profile one critical clay value predicted spectra yields B. distinguishing feature soilKey versus hypothetical LLM-driven classifier. Provenance survives entire pipeline; output never produced every input lab-measured.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Architecture, taxonomy interpretation, per-RSG canonical fixtures: Hugo Rodrigues Machado (Universidade Federal Rural Rio de Janeiro, UFRRJ — Departamento de Solos). Builds aqp (Beaudette et al., USDA-NRCS) pedological data structures, SoilTaxonomy (Beaudette et al.) USDA lookup tables, Open Soil Spectral Library consortium spectral backbone, ISRIC SoilGrids WoSIS. deterministic-key / multimodal-extraction / spectroscopy / spatial-prior separation documented detail ARCHITECTURE.md, per-release scope tracked commit history (NEWS.md v1.0 onwards).

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Education without ethics rhetoric. Power without reflection violence. Made ❤️ Hugo Rodrigues Soil Science      Status: CRAN-ready, v0.9.12. R CMD check ---cran returns 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 2 expected NOTEs (New submission + PROJ env-, gone CRAN’s farm). GitHub Actions green across 5 OS×R matrix. three classification systems wired end--end deepest categorical level — WRB 2022 (32 RSGs + qualifiers + supplementary + specifiers), SiBCS 5ª ed. (Ordem → Subordem → Grande Grupo → Subgrupo → Família, ~1 200 classes), USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup, ~1 700 classes). v0.9.12 ships maximal WoSIS GraphQL coverage + tier-stratified reporting + forensic walkthrough showing 0/5 Tier-1 misses genuine classifier failures (rest documented WoSIS schema gaps). v0.9.11 added classify_from_documents() high-level VLM one-liner, default Gemma 4 (gemma4:e4b) Ollama, plus 1.1 MB data(ossl_demo_sa) synthetic OSSL artefact. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19930112 (resolves latest version Zenodo). Roadmap ARCHITECTURE.md §12; per-release changes NEWS.md; CRAN submission instructions inst/cran-submission/HOW_TO_SUBMIT.md.

title: Horizonte B incipiente (SiBCS Cap 2, p 59-61; v0.7) — B_incipiente

what: Horizonte B latossolico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 57-59; v0.7 strict) — B_latossolico
title: Horizonte B latossolico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 57-59; v0.7 strict) — B_latossolico
text: Adicionalmente ferralic (WRB), o B latossolico SiBCS exige: Espessura minima de 50 cm; Textura francoarenosa ou mais fina; Estrutura granular muito pequena/pequena ou em blocos         subangulares fraco/moderado; < 5% volume mostrando estrutura da rocha original; Ki \<= 2.2 (geralmente \<= 2.0); Cerosidade maximo pouca e fraca. v0.7 enforce thickness, texture, e ausencia de estrutura primaria herdada via designation e clay; Ki/Kr quantitativos sao v0.8 (precisa de SiO2/Al2O3 lab-data nao schema).

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: Returned classify_wrb2022 (future classify_sibcs). Carries full decision trace — RSGs tested, passed, failed, indeterminate missing data — plus assigned class, qualifiers, ambiguities (RSGs nearly satisfied), missing data refine result, provenance-aware evidence grade, biogeographical prior-based warnings.

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: system Character. "WRB 2022" "SiBCS 5". name Character. Full classification name qualifiers (e.g. "Rhodic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric)"). rsg_or_order Character. Bare RSG (WRB) order (SiBCS), e.g. "Ferralsols". qualifiers List. Principal supplementary qualifiers canonical order. trace List. One element per RSG tested (key order), code, name, passed, evidence, missing. ambiguities List. RSGs came close passing — useful hints follow-measurements. missing_data Character vector. Attributes whose measurement refine resolve result. evidence_grade Character. "", "B", "C", "D", NA_character_. prior_check List NULL. Result spatial-prior sanity check (consistent / inconsistent / run). warnings Character vector. Free-form warnings.

title: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome of a diagnostic test — DiagnosticResult
text: Returned every WRB SiBCS diagnostic function (e.g. argic, ferralic, mollic). DiagnosticResult never reduces bare TRUE/FALSE — always carries () layers satisfied criteria, (b) per-sub-test evidence, (c) attributes required missing, (d) literature reference diagnostic definition. passed TRUE/FALSE/NA; NA means test evaluated critical attributes missing. three-valued semantics propagates rule engine — indeterminate test silently fail.

title: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome of a diagnostic test — DiagnosticResult
text: name Character. Name diagnostic (e.g. "argic"). passed Logical. TRUE, FALSE, NA. layers Integer vector. Indices horizons satisfy diagnostic. evidence Named list. Sub-test results, list least passed, layers, missing. missing Character vector. Attribute names needed NA. reference Character. Literature citation diagnostic. notes Character. Free-form notes (interpretation choices, edge cases hit).

what: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider

text: Mock VLM provider testing Mock VLM provider testing

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
text: stand-ellmer chat object. Exposes $chat(prompt, ...) method, instead calling model pops next response pre-loaded queue. Designed testthat unit tests exercise extraction logic without API keys network access. call $chat() returns next element responses list. call number matches validation_error_at, response replaced deliberately malformed JSON string, allowing tests exercise retry--validation-failure path implemented validate_or_retry.

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
code: good_json <- '{"horizons": [...]}' mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(responses = list(good_json)) result <- mock$chat("any prompt")  # returns good_json  # Simulate one validation error before success. mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(   responses = list("not really json", good_json),   validation_error_at = NULL  # already invalid as-is )  # Or force an attempt to be invalid via the helper. mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(   responses = list(good_json, good_json),   validation_error_at = 1L )

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
text: use, mock exposes $call_count (integer) $prompts_received (list every prompt passed $chat()), lets tests assert retry prompts include previous validation error.

text: responses List canned responses (character scalars R objects JSON-serialised). validation_error_at Optional integer; call number matches, returned text replaced malformed JSON string. call_count Integer counter (0 call). prompts_received List recording every prompt passed $chat().

text: PedonRecord: structured representation single pedon PedonRecord: structured representation single pedon

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: central data carrier soilKey. PedonRecord bundles everything know one soil profile: site metadata, horizons table (fixed canonical schema — see horizon_column_spec), optional Vis-NIR/MIR spectra, profile photographs, source documents, provenance log records, per (horizon, attribute) pair, value came (measured, extracted_vlm, predicted_spectra, inferred_prior, user_assumed). diagnostic functions (argic, ferralic, mollic, ...) consume PedonRecord directly. provenance log allows final ClassificationResult assign meaningful evidence grade.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: site List. Site-level metadata: lat, lon, crs (default 4326), date, country, elevation_m, slope_pct, aspect_deg, landform, parent_material, land_use, vegetation, drainage_class, plus arbitrary id. horizons data.table canonical horizon schema. spectra List optional vnir matrix (rows = horizons, cols = wavelengths nm), mir matrix, metadata list. images List named lists describing profile photographs. documents List named lists describing source documents. provenance data.table columns horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence, notes.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: Validate record soil-physical sanity rules. Checks: top < bottom every horizon; overlapping depths; clay+silt+sand sum 100 ± 2 three reported; pH values plausible (1..12); CEC >= sum exchangeable bases (Ca, Mg, K, Na); Munsell value/chroma plausible ranges; coarse fragments percent [0, 100]; OC geographic ranges. Returns list valid, errors, warnings, n_horizons.

title: Abrupt textural difference (WRB 2022 Ch 3.2.1) — abrupt_textural_difference
text: Sharp clay-content increase two superimposed mineral layers meeting : underlying clay \>= 15% thickness \>= 7.5 cm; underlying starts \>= 10 cm mineral soil surface; underlying , vs overlying: 2x clay overlying < 20%,         \>= 20pp (absolute) clay overlying \>= 20%; transitional layer, , \<= 2 cm. v0.3.3 enforces criteria 1, 2, 3. transitional-layer check deferred (canonical horizon schema carry "transitional" marker; can added later via boundary_distinctness inspection).

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: Tests andic property complex – volcanic-ash-derived allophanic / imogolitic / Al-humus material. Diagnostic Andosols. Two alternative qualifying paths per WRB 2022 Ch 3.2: Al-Fe oxalate + low BD:         (Al_ox + 0.5*Fe_ox) >= min_alfe (default 2.0%)         bulk_density <= max_bd (default 0.9 g/cm^3)         layer. Phosphate retention: phosphate_retention_pct         >= min_p_retention (default 70%). Either path qualifies. volcanic-glass criterion separate vitric_properties diagnostic; Andosols key (andic vitric) RSG-gate level (andosol).

title: Anhydrous conditions (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — anhydrous_conditions_usda
text: Required characteristics: Mean annual soil temperature <= 0 C; Layer 10-70 cm soil temperature < 5 C throughout year; ice-impregnated permafrost layer; One : Dry (>= 1500 kPa) 1/2+ soil 1/2+ time             0 C; Rupture-resistance class loose slightly hard             throughout temp <= 0 C (except pedogenically             cemented). Implementation (v0.8.x): Uses permafrost_temp_C schema flag layers freezing; checks rupture_resistance "loose" / "soft" / "slightly hard" 10-70 cm layer. Precipitation criterion deferred v0.9 (climatic data).

title: Anthric horizons (WRB 2022) — anthric_horizons
text: Tests five anthropogenic surface horizons recognised WRB 2022 (hortic, irragric, plaggic, pretic, terric). Diagnostic Anthrosols. Two alternative paths qualify: Designation: layer's designation contains one         hortic|irragric|plaggic|pretic|terric. Property-based: surface layer (top_cm <= 5)         least min_thickness_cm cm thick (default 20)         elevated dark colour (Munsell value moist <=         max_munsell_value, default 4) elevated         plant-available P (p_mehlich3_mg_kg >=         min_p_mg_kg, default 50). Either path qualifies.

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: Tests whether horizon meets argic horizon criteria per Chapter 3 WRB 2022 (4th edition). Argic subsurface horizon distinctly higher clay content overlying horizon, qualified three depth-conditional clay-increase rules; must also texture sandy loam finer, satisfy minimum thickness, exhibit albeluvic glossic features (direct profile Retisol path).

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: Sub-tests called (list passed, layers, missing, details, notes): test_clay_increase_argic – three-pronged         WRB 2022 clay-increase rule. test_minimum_thickness – thickness >= 7.5 cm         (configurable via min_thickness). test_texture_argic – texture sandy loam         finer (silt + 2 * clay >= 30). test_not_albeluvic – excludes profiles glossic         tongues (Retisol path). v0.1 limitations: clay-increase distance (<= 30 cm vertical, <= 15 cm abrupt textural change) yet enforced; scheduled v0.2 depends horizon boundary descriptions.

what: Atividade da fracao argila (SiBCS Cap 1, p 30) — atividade_argila_alta
title: Atividade da fracao argila (SiBCS Cap 1, p 30) — atividade_argila_alta
text: Calcula atividade da fracao argila Ta = CEC * 1000 / argila (em cmolc/kg de argila, sem correcao para carbono) por horizonte e classifica como **alta (Ta)** se >= 27 cmolc/kg argila ou **baixa (Tb)** se < 27. Nao se aplica texturas areia / areia franca.

title: Atividade da fracao argila (SiBCS Cap 1, p 30) — atividade_argila_alta

title: Carater acrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 31) — carater_acrico
text: Indica solos com balanca de cargas predominante eletropositiva ou eletricamente neutra. Discrimina Latossolos Acricos / Acriferricos 3o nivel (Cap 10).

title: Carater acrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 31) — carater_acrico
Carater acrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 31) — carater_acrico
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_ecec_clay Limite superior de CECef/argila em cmolc/kg argila (default 1.5). min_delta_ph Limite inferior de \(\Delta pH\) (default 0).

title: Carater acrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 31) — carater_acrico
text: DiagnosticResult; passed = TRUE se         pelo menos um horizonte B satisfaz ambos os criterios.

title: Carater acrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 31) — carater_acrico
text: Criterios canonicos (todos verificados em horizontes B): 1. \(\Delta pH = pH(KCl) - pH(H_2O) \ge 0\)   2. CECef por kg de argila \(\le\) 1.5 cmolc/kg argila

title: Carater acrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 31) — carater_acrico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 1, p 31; Cap 10             (Latossolos), pp 173-176.

title: Carater alitico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 32) — carater_alitico
text: Critérios canônicos: Al(extr) >= 4 cmolc/kg solo, saturacao por aluminio [100 * Al / (S + Al)] >= 50%, e saturacao por bases V < 50%. Avaliado horizonte B (ou C, na ausencia de B).

title: Carater alitico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 32) — carater_alitico
Carater alitico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 32) — carater_alitico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_al Numeric threshold option (see Details). min_al_sat Numeric threshold option (see Details). max_v Numeric threshold option (see Details).

title: Carater arenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_arenico
text: Solos com textura arenosa (clay% < max_clay_pct, default 15%) desde superficie ate uma profundidade entre min_depth_cm e max_depth_cm (default 50-100 cm). Discrimina os Subgrupos arenicos de Argissolos (Cap 5: PAC, PA, PV, PVA) e Neossolos (Cap 12).

title: Carater arenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_arenico
Carater arenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_arenico
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_clay_pct Limite superior de % argila para "arenoso" (default 15 = areia / areia franca). min_depth_cm Profundidade minima boundary (default 50). max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima boundary (default 100).

title: Carater arenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_arenico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater arenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_arenico
text: Implementacao: ordena horizontes por top_cm, identifica o PRIMEIRO horizonte com clay_pct >= max_clay_pct, e verifica que () todos os horizontes acima desse boundary sao arenosos (sem camada argilosa intercalada acima) e (b) o boundary (top_cm) cai intervalo [min_depth_cm, max_depth_cm]. Para "espessarenicos" (boundary 100-200 cm), use carater_espessarenico (planejado v0.7.4.B.3).

title: Carater arenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_arenico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 5 (Argissolos),             pp 120-138.

title: Carater argiluvico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 6) — carater_argiluvico
text: Solos com B textural (B_textural) em posicao NAO diagnostica para Argissolos, dentro de max_depth_cm. Discrimina os Subgrupos argissolicos de Cambissolos (Cap 6 CX 4.7.8, 4.10.5).

title: Carater argiluvico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 6) — carater_argiluvico
Carater argiluvico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 6) — carater_argiluvico
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Default 150 cm.

title: Carater argiluvico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 6) — carater_argiluvico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater argiluvico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 6) — carater_argiluvico
text: Implementacao v0.7.5: requer B_textural passa em alguma camada com top_cm \(<\) max_depth_cm. Distingue-se de Argissolo pleno por contexto: chamado dentro de Cambissolos onde B incipiente (B_incipiente) ja definiu ordem como Cambissolo.

title: Carater argiluvico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 6) — carater_argiluvico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 1; Cap 6, p 153.

title: Carater cambissolico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_cambissolico
text: Solos com B incipiente (B_incipiente) abaixo horizonte hístico (H/O) ou . Discrimina os Subgrupos cambissolicos de Organossolos Folicos (Cap 14, pp 247-248): Folicos Fibricos / Hemicos / Sapricos cambissolicos.

title: Carater cambissolico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_cambissolico
Carater cambissolico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_cambissolico
text: pedon PedonRecord.

title: Carater cambissolico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_cambissolico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater cambissolico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_cambissolico
text: Implementado como uma interseccao de duas condicoes: B_incipiente passa em ao menos um horizonte Esse horizonte B incipiente esta abaixo de um horizonte         H/O (hístico) ou Em pedons sem H/O ou acima B incipiente, o teste falha (B incipiente isolado nao caracteriza Organossolo Cambissolico).

title: Carater cambissolico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_cambissolico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 14, pp 247-248.

title: Carater cambissolico (Argissolos – Cap 5) — carater_cambissolico_arg
text: Solos com 4% ou mais de minerais alteraveis visiveis E/OU 5% ou mais de fragmentos de rocha (coarse_fragments_pct) horizonte B (exclusive BC ou B/C), dentro de max_depth_cm. Discrimina os Subgrupos cambissolicos de Argissolos PA (Cap 5, p 126) – DISTINTO carater_cambissolico (Cap 14 Organossolos Folicos: B incipiente abaixo de histico/).

title: Carater cambissolico (Argissolos – Cap 5) — carater_cambissolico_arg
Carater cambissolico (Argissolos – Cap 5) — carater_cambissolico_arg
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_coarse_pct Default 5% volume. max_depth_cm Default 150 cm.

title: Carater cambissolico (Argissolos – Cap 5) — carater_cambissolico_arg
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater cambissolico (Argissolos – Cap 5) — carater_cambissolico_arg
text: Implementacao v0.7.4 (aproximacao): apenas coarse_fragments_pct \(\ge\) min_coarse_pct (default 5) eh testado. O criterio "minerais alteraveis visiveis" exigiria campo adicional schema (e.g. weatherable_minerals_pct) que sera adicionado em release futura. Documentado como limitacao conhecida.

title: Carater cambissolico (Argissolos – Cap 5) — carater_cambissolico_arg
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 5, p 126.

title: Carater carbonatico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 33) — carater_carbonatico
text: >= 150 g/kg (15%) de CaCO3 equivalente em qualquer forma de segregacao (incl. nodulos, concrecoes). Excludente: nao satisfaz aos requisitos de horizonte calcico.

title: Carater carbonatico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 33) — carater_carbonatico
Carater carbonatico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 33) — carater_carbonatico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_caco3_pct Limite de CaCO3 (default 15%). max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima (top_cm) em que camadas qualificam (default NULL = sem restricao). SiBCS Cap 14 Subgrupos usam max_depth_cm = 150.

what: Carater chernossolico (SiBCS Cap 5; A chernozemico + Ta alta) — carater_chernossolico
title: Carater chernossolico (SiBCS Cap 5; A chernozemico + Ta alta) — carater_chernossolico
text: Solos com horizonte chernozemico (horizonte_A_chernozemico) E atividade da argila \(\ge\) min_ta (default 20 cmolc/kg argila) na maior parte dos primeiros 100 cm B (inclusive BA). Discrimina os Subgrupos chernossolicos de Argissolos (Cap 5: PV, PVA).

title: Carater chernossolico (SiBCS Cap 5; A chernozemico + Ta alta) — carater_chernossolico
Carater chernossolico (SiBCS Cap 5; A chernozemico + Ta alta) — carater_chernossolico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_ta Threshold de atividade da argila (default 20).

title: Carater chernossolico (SiBCS Cap 5; A chernozemico + Ta alta) — carater_chernossolico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater chernossolico (SiBCS Cap 5; A chernozemico + Ta alta) — carater_chernossolico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 5, p 134; Cap 7             (Chernossolos).

title: Carater coeso (SiBCS Cap 1, pp 32-33) — carater_coeso
text: Solos com horizontes coesos: muito duros extremamente duros quando secos, friaveis firmes quando umidos, decorrentes empacotamento das particulas e/ou cimentacao. Discrimina os Grandes Grupos Distrocoesos / Eutrocoesos de Argissolos (Cap 5, pp 117-119) e Latossolos (Cap 10).

title: Carater coeso (SiBCS Cap 1, pp 32-33) — carater_coeso
Carater coeso (SiBCS Cap 1, pp 32-33) — carater_coeso
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima onde camadas qualificam (default 150, conforme SiBCS Cap 5: "dentro de 150 cm partir da superficie").

title: Carater coeso (SiBCS Cap 1, pp 32-33) — carater_coeso
text: DiagnosticResult; passed = TRUE se ao         menos uma camada (com textura suficiente) atende aos dois         criterios de consistencia.

title: Carater coeso (SiBCS Cap 1, pp 32-33) — carater_coeso
text: Criterios canonicos: rupture_resistance \(\\){"hard",         "extremely hard"} (em estado seco) consistence_moist \(\\){"friable", "firm"}         (em estado umido) Excluido: textura areia / areia franca (clay_pct < 15%)

title: Carater coeso (SiBCS Cap 1, pp 32-33) — carater_coeso
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 1, pp 32-33;             Cap 5 (Argissolos), pp 117-119.

title: Carater durico (SiBCS Cap 1) — carater_durico
text: Solos com endurecimento por cimentacao parcial de silica (SiO2), insuficiente para qualificar como horizonte durico (duripa) completo. Detectado quando:

title: Carater durico (SiBCS Cap 1) — carater_durico
Carater durico (SiBCS Cap 1) — carater_durico
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima onde camadas qualificam (default 150, conforme SiBCS Cap 5: "dentro de 150 cm").

title: Carater durico (SiBCS Cap 1) — carater_durico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater durico (SiBCS Cap 1) — carater_durico
text: duripan_pct > 0 (presenca de noduros / concrecoes         de silica), cementation_class \(\\){"weakly", "moderately"}         (cimentacao fraca moderada, NAO indurada/strongly). Discrimina os Subgrupos duricos / abrupticos duricos de Argissolos Acinzentados (Cap 5 PAC) e Latossolos com caracter durico (Cap 10).

title: Carater durico (SiBCS Cap 1) — carater_durico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 1; Cap 5 (Argissolos             Acinzentados Distrocoesos abrupticos duricos), p 120.

what: Carater ebanico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 7 e Cap 17) — carater_ebanico
title: Carater ebanico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 7 e Cap 17) — carater_ebanico
text: Cor preta uniforme (value \(\le\) 3 e chroma \(\le\) 2 em umido) em TODO o horizonte B + atividade da argila alta (Ta) + saturacao por bases V% \(\ge\) 65. Discrimina Chernossolos Ebanicos (Cap 7) e Vertissolos Ebanicos (Cap 17) 2o nivel.

title: Carater ebanico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 7 e Cap 17) — carater_ebanico
Carater ebanico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 7 e Cap 17) — carater_ebanico

title: Carater espessarenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_espessarenico
text: Textura arenosa (clay% < max_clay_pct) da superficie ate boundary em [100, 200] cm. Variante "espessa" carater_arenico.

title: Carater espessarenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_espessarenico
Carater espessarenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_espessarenico
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_clay_pct Limite superior de % argila (default 15). min_depth_cm Profundidade minima boundary (default 100). max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima boundary (default 200).

title: Carater espessarenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_espessarenico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater espessarenico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_espessarenico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 5, pp 130-131.

what: Carater espodico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8) — carater_espodico
title: Carater espodico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8) — carater_espodico
text: Evidencia iluvial de Al / Fe / materia organica em camada de pelo menos 2,5 cm de espessura, em quantidade insuficiente para qualificar como horizonte B espodico (B_espodico), mas suficiente para indicar espodicidade incipiente. Usado em Cambissolos / Argissolos / Plintossolos espodicos (Caps 5, 6 e 16) e em Espodossolos rasos (Cap 8).

title: Carater espodico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8) — carater_espodico
Carater espodico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8) — carater_espodico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Espessura minima da camada espodica incipiente em cm (default 2,5). min_oc_pct OC% minimo em camada candidata (default 0,5).

title: Carater espodico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8) — carater_espodico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater espodico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8) — carater_espodico
text: Diferenca para B_espodico: thickness >= 2,5 cm em vez de exigir o gate completo de espessura espodica; OC >= 0,5% em vez gate de iluviacao quantitativa; sinais de iluviacao Fe/Al (al_ox_pct ou fe_ox_pct ou fe_dcb_pct).

title: Carater espodico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8) — carater_espodico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 1, p 35; Cap 8             (Espodossolos), pp 156-160.

what: Carater B espodico profundo (SiBCS Cap 8) — carater_espodico_profundo
title: Carater B espodico profundo (SiBCS Cap 8) — carater_espodico_profundo
text: Solos com horizonte B espodico cujo top_cm esta entre min_top_cm (default 200) e max_top_cm (default 400). Discrimina os Grandes Grupos Hiperespessos / Hidro-hiperespessos de Espodossolos (Cap 8 1.1, 1.3, 2.1, 2.3, 3.1, 3.3).

title: Carater B espodico profundo (SiBCS Cap 8) — carater_espodico_profundo
Carater B espodico profundo (SiBCS Cap 8) — carater_espodico_profundo
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_top_cm Default 200. max_top_cm Default 400.

title: Carater B espodico profundo (SiBCS Cap 8) — carater_espodico_profundo
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater B espodico profundo (SiBCS Cap 8) — carater_espodico_profundo
text: Implementacao: chama carater_espodico e filtra por top_cm intervalo [min_top_cm, max_top_cm].

title: Carater B espodico profundo (SiBCS Cap 8) — carater_espodico_profundo
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 8, pp 165-168.

title: Carater eutrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35) — carater_eutrico
text: Distinto de "eutrofico": exige pH(H2O) >= 5.7 conjugado com S (soma de bases) >= 2.0 cmolc/kg solo dentro da secao de controle.

title: Carater eutrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35) — carater_eutrico
Carater eutrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35) — carater_eutrico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_pH Numeric threshold option (see Details). min_s Numeric threshold option (see Details).

what: Carater ferrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5 e Cap 10) — carater_ferrico
title: Carater ferrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5 e Cap 10) — carater_ferrico
text: Teor de Fe2O3 (pelo ataque sulfurico-NaOH) entre 180 e 360 g/kg de solo (= 18%-36% mass) na maior parte dos primeiros 100 cm horizonte B. Acima de 360 g/kg = "perferrico" (nao implementado aqui). Discrimina os Grandes Grupos Eutroferricos / Distroferricos / Aluminoferricos de Latossolos (Cap 10), Argissolos (Cap 5 Eutroferricos) e Cambissolos (Cap 6 Aluminoferricos).

title: Carater ferrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5 e Cap 10) — carater_ferrico
Carater ferrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5 e Cap 10) — carater_ferrico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_fe2o3_pct Limite inferior de Fe2O3 sulfurico em % mass (default 18 = 180 g/kg). max_fe2o3_pct Limite superior (exclusivo) em % mass (default 36 = 360 g/kg). max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima de B avaliado (default 100).

title: Carater ferrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5 e Cap 10) — carater_ferrico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater ferrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5 e Cap 10) — carater_ferrico
text: Implementacao v0.7.4: testa se algum horizonte B dentro de max_depth_cm atende ao intervalo. SiBCS estrito ("na maior parte de") seria uma media ponderada por espessura – refinamento planejado para v0.7.5.

title: Carater ferrico (SiBCS Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5 e Cap 10) — carater_ferrico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 1, p 35; Cap 5             (Argissolos Eutroferricos, p 118); Cap 10 (Latossolos).

Carater gleissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; horizonte_glei em posicao nao-Gleissolo) — carater_gleissolico

Discrimina os Grandes Grupos Hidromorficos / Hidro-hiperespessos de Espodossolos (Cap 8 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, 2.2, 3.1, 3.2).

title: Carater espesso-humico (SiBCS Cap 5, p 119) — carater_humico_espesso
text: Solos com horizonte humico e conteudo de carbono >= min_oc_pct (default 1% = 10 g/kg) extendendo-se ate min_depth_cm (default 80 cm) ou mais de profundidade. Discrimina os Subgrupos "espesso-humicos" de Argissolos Bruno-Acinzentados Ta Aluminicos (Cap 5 PBAC 1.1.2) – camadas humosas espessas tipicas de Argissolos RS.

title: Carater espesso-humico (SiBCS Cap 5, p 119) — carater_humico_espesso
Carater espesso-humico (SiBCS Cap 5, p 119) — carater_humico_espesso
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_oc_pct Limite inferior de OC% nas camadas inferiores (default 1.0 = 10 g/kg). min_depth_cm Profundidade minima de extensao C alto (default 80 cm).

title: Carater espesso-humico (SiBCS Cap 5, p 119) — carater_humico_espesso
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater espesso-humico (SiBCS Cap 5, p 119) — carater_humico_espesso
text: Implementacao: requer (1) horizonte_A_humico passa (2) ha camada com oc_pct >= min_oc_pct cuja bottom_cm >= min_depth_cm.

title: Carater espesso-humico (SiBCS Cap 5, p 119) — carater_humico_espesso
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 5 (Argissolos), p 119.

title: Carater latossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_latossolico
text: Solos com horizonte B latossolico (B_latossolico) abaixo horizonte B textural (B_textural), dentro de max_depth_cm (default 150 cm). Discrimina os Subgrupos latossolicos de Argissolos (Cap 5: PAC, PA, PV, PVA) – transicao entre Argissolo e Latossolo dentro mesmo perfil.

title: Carater latossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_latossolico

title: Carater latossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_latossolico

what: Carater luvissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; Ta + S alta) — carater_luvissolico
title: Carater luvissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; Ta + S alta) — carater_luvissolico
text: Solos com atividade da argila \(\ge\) min_ta (default 20 cmolc/kg argila) E soma de bases (S) \(\ge\) min_s (default 5 cmolc/kg solo), ambos na maior parte dos primeiros 100 cm horizonte B. Discrimina os Subgrupos luvissolicos de Argissolos (Cap 5: PV, PVA).

title: Carater luvissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; Ta + S alta) — carater_luvissolico
Carater luvissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; Ta + S alta) — carater_luvissolico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_ta Threshold de atividade da argila em cmolc/kg argila (default 20). min_s Threshold de S em cmolc/kg solo (default 5). max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima de B avaliado (default 100).

title: Carater luvissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; Ta + S alta) — carater_luvissolico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater luvissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; Ta + S alta) — carater_luvissolico
text: Note: o threshold de Ta para "luvissolico" e 20 (vs 27 para atividade_argila_alta canonico). S = Ca + Mg + K + Na trocaveis.

title: Carater luvissolico (SiBCS Cap 5; Ta + S alta) — carater_luvissolico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 5, p 134; Cap 11             (Luvissolos).

title: Carater nitossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_nitossolico
text: Solos com morfologia (estrutura e cerosidade) semelhante aos Nitossolos, mas diferindo por apresentar relacao textural B/\(>\) 1,5 OU policromia (multiplas matizes Munsell em horizontes B) dentro de max_depth_cm cm. Discrimina os Subgrupos nitossolicos de Argissolos (Cap 5: PV, PVA).

title: Carater nitossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_nitossolico
Carater nitossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_nitossolico
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_b_a_ratio Razao maxima B/para Nitossolos (default 1.5); Argissolos nitossolicos tem ratio > 1.5. max_depth_cm Profundidade maxima B avaliado (default 150).

title: Carater nitossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_nitossolico
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Carater nitossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_nitossolico
text: Implementacao v0.7.4 (aproximacao): cerosidade \(\ge\) comum + moderada, Razao textural B/> max_b_a_ratio (default 1.5),         policromia (\(\ge\) 2 matizes distintos em B).

title: Carater nitossolico (SiBCS Cap 5) — carater_nitossolico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 5, pp 129-131; Cap 13.

title: Carater perferrico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 6 CX Perferricos) — carater_perferrico

title: Carater placico (SiBCS Cap 5; horizonte placico cementado por Fe/Mn) — carater_placico
text: Camada cimentada por Fe/Mn (geralmente fina, 1-25 mm), detectada via cementation_class %% {"strongly", "indurated"} dentro de max_depth_cm. Discrimina os Subgrupos placicos de Argissolos PA (Cap 5).

what: Carater sombrico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 5 PV) — carater_sombrico
title: Carater sombrico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 5 PV) — carater_sombrico
text: Camada subsuperficial com acumulacao iluvial de materia organica, caracterizada por cores escuras (munsell_value_moist \(\le\) 4, munsell_chroma_moist \(\le\) 3) e oc_pct \(\ge\) 0.5%, em B abaixo de /E. Distinto de B espodico por nao requerer iluviacao Al/Fe. Tipico de solos altitudinais (planaltos sul-brasileiros). Discrimina o Subgrupo sombricos de Argissolos Vermelhos Aluminicos (Cap 5 PV 4.2.6).

title: Carater sombrico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 5 PV) — carater_sombrico
code: carater_sombrico(   pedon,   max_value = 4,   max_chroma = 3,   min_oc_pct = 0.5,   max_depth_cm = 150 )

title: Carater sombrico (SiBCS Cap 1; Cap 5 PV) — carater_sombrico

title: Carater terrico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_terrico
text: Solos com horizontes ou camadas constituidos por materiais minerais (horizonte , Ag, Big e/ou Cg), com espessura cumulativa \(\ge\) min_thickness_cm dentro de within_depth_cm da superficie solo. Discrimina os Subgrupos terricos de Organossolos (Cap 14, pp 245-250) e Cambissolos terricos (Cap 6).

title: Carater terrico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_terrico
Carater terrico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_terrico
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness_cm Espessura cumulativa minima de material mineral (default 30 cm). within_depth_cm Profundidade de busca (default 100 cm).

title: Carater terrico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_terrico
text: DiagnosticResult; passed = TRUE se         soma da espessura dos horizontes minerais (truncada em         within_depth_cm) \(\ge\)         min_thickness_cm.

title: Carater terrico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_terrico
text: Padroes de designacao reconhecidos para horizonte mineral: , Ap, (mineral superficial) Ag (mineral hidromorfico) Big, Bg (B mineral hidromorfico) Cg (C mineral hidromorfico) C, Cr, Crf (mineral subsuperficial) Excluidos somatorio: horizontes histicos (H*, O*) e horizontes cementados puros sem material mineral.

title: Carater terrico (SiBCS Cap 14) — carater_terrico
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 14, p 246             (subgrupos terricos de Organossolos).

what: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: Given Vis-NIR (MIR) spectrum OSSL reference library enriched WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels, returns K spectrally similar profiles plus probabilistic class prediction aggregated labels.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
code: classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum,   ossl_library,   system = c("wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda"),   k = 25L,   preprocess = "snv+sg1",   region = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: spectrum Numeric vector 1-row matrix (query spectrum). Must align (preprocessing) column space ossl_library$Xr. ossl_library list Xr (numeric matrix, rows = OSSL training profiles, cols = wavelengths) Yr (data frame keyed property; must include column named wrb_rsg / sibcs_ordem / usda_order labels aggregate ). ossl_library may also carry lat lon columns Yr regional filter. system One "wrb2022" (default), "sibcs", "usda". Controls label column Yr aggregated. k Number nearest neighbours (default 25). preprocess Pre-processing pipeline; passed preprocess_spectra. Default "snv+sg1". region Optional list(lat, lon, radius_km) regional filter ossl_library$Yr$lat / lon. verbose Emit cli summary.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: list three elements: distribution data.table columns           class, n_neighbours, probability           (= n_neighbours / k), sorted probability. neighbours data.table one row per           neighbour (top K), columns rank, distance,           class, plus columns present           ossl_library$Yr. query query metadata (system, k,           region filter, n_library_rows, n_filtered).

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: **spectral analogy** classifier. replace deterministic key classify_wrb2022 / classify_sibcs / classify_usda; instead provides high-prior "expected class" user lab data, reducing search space collecting confirming attributes.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: default compute distances PLS scores (matching resemble / OSSL recipe), PLS components fit OSSL reference Yr matrix. resemble unavailable, fall back PCA scores stats::prcomp preprocessed Xr – defensible--simpler heuristic.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: Optional lat / lon / radius_km arguments filter OSSL library profiles within radius_km (great-circle) query location computing distances. implements "biome-aware" use case architecture document calls : Cerrado profile class inferred spectral neighbours Boreal taiga.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Toy run against the bundled demo library (synthetic): data(ossl_demo_sa) # Inject a fake label column for the demo (real OSSL has it): ossl_demo_sa$Yr$wrb_rsg <- sample(c("FR", "AC", "LX", "AL"),                                     nrow(ossl_demo_sa$Yr),                                     replace = TRUE) query <- ossl_demo_sa$Xr[1, ] res <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(query, ossl_demo_sa,                                         k = 10) res$distribution    # ranked classes res$neighbours      # the 10 most similar profiles } # }

what: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: Highest-level entry point soilKey VLM pipeline. Given soil-description PDF / profile-wall photograph, function:

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
code: classify_from_documents(   pdf = NULL,   image = NULL,   fieldsheet = NULL,   pedon = NULL,   provider = "auto",   model = NULL,   systems = c("wrb", "sibcs", "usda"),   report = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: pdf Optional path soil-description PDF. image Optional path profile-wall image (JPG / PNG); supplied, Munsell extraction attempted configured provider. fieldsheet Optional path site-metadata field sheet (image PDF). pedon Optional existing PedonRecord; supplied, function fills fields VLM extraction can fill (subject provenance-authority order). provider Either provider name passed vlm_provider (default "ollama") pre-built ellmer chat object (want full control system_prompt, api_key, ...). model Optional model identifier; passed vlm_provider() provider string. Defaults per-provider default default_model. systems Character vector listing classification systems run; subset c("wrb", "sibcs", "usda"). Default: three. report Optional output path self-contained report (.html .pdf). supplied, report called classification results + pedon. Default NULL (report file). overwrite merging extracted values existing pedon, allow VLM-extracted attributes clobber already-recorded ones. Default FALSE – provenance authority order (measured > extracted_vlm) enforced PedonRecord$add_measurement(). verbose Emit cli progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: list elements: pedon (mutated) PedonRecord. classifications Named list three           ClassificationResult objects keyed           wrb, sibcs, usda. report Path rendered report file (          report = ... supplied), else NULL. provider chat-provider object actually used           (useful downstream debugging cost accounting).

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: Constructs vision-language provider chat object via         vlm_provider (defaults local Ollama         Gemma 4 edge institutional independence data         sovereignty). Extracts horizons pdf via         extract_horizons_from_pdf, Munsell colours         image via         extract_munsell_from_photo, site         metadata fieldsheet via         extract_site_from_fieldsheet. Every         extracted attribute stamped source =         "extracted_vlm" PedonRecord's provenance log. Runs three deterministic keys         (classify_wrb2022,         classify_sibcs,         classify_usda). VLM never classifies –         package's architectural invariant preserved. Optionally renders one-pager HTML / PDF report via         report. least one pdf, image fieldsheet must supplied; can also pass existing partially-filled PedonRecord via pedon let function fill gaps.

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: default provider = "ollama" runs entire VLM pipeline user's machine via Gemma 4 (edge variant, ~3 GB, multimodal text+image). part soil description, photograph field sheet ever leaves local network. recommended configuration governmental surveys, indigenous land studies, unpublished research data; also makes pipeline reproducible without internet connection. Cloud providers ("anthropic", "openai", "google") remain one argument away right call.

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: VLM never classifies. Every extracted value carries         source = "extracted_vlm"; deterministic keys         consume resulting PedonRecord unaware         value obtained. Provenance preserved end--end.         evidence_grade         ClassificationResult reflects whether decisive         attributes came measured,         predicted_spectra, extracted_vlm,         inferred_prior, user_assumed –         caller always knows robust classification . Authority order enforced. pre-existing         measured value never silently overwritten         later extracted_vlm value (unless         overwrite = TRUE).

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # The simplest possible end-to-end call -- local Gemma 4 edge. res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html" ) res$classifications$wrb$name #> "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  # Cloud provider for a one-shot, production run res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   provider = "anthropic" )  # Different Gemma 4 size on Ollama res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   provider = "ollama",   model    = "gemma4:31b" ) } # }

what: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs
title: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs

Quando subordem ainda nao tem bloco de Grandes Grupos, ou quando nenhum Grande Grupo passa (e nao ha catch-default), classificacao para 2o nivel.

O label textual da Familia aparece em $trace$familia_label, e lista de FamilyAttributes em $trace$familia.

title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia

Outras dimensoes (prefixos epi/ meso/endo, saturacao de bases, alico, mineralogia, atividade da argila, oxidos de ferro, andico, especificos de Organossolos) adicionadas em sub-commits subsequentes.

title: Cryic conditions (WRB 2022) — cryic_conditions
text: Tests whether continuous frozen / permafrost material occurs within upper max_top_cm. Two alternative paths qualify per WRB 2022: Permafrost temperature: layer top_cm <=         max_top_cm (default 100)         permafrost_temp_C <= max_temp_C (default 0 C). Designation pattern: layer top_cm <=         max_top_cm designation containing suffix         "f" (frozen) matching "^Cf" / "perma".         Used fallback temperature field         pedon (typical legacy survey data). Either path qualifies. Diagnostic Cryosols.
