text: Fetches region-filtered subset Open Soil Spectral Library (Sanderman et al. 2024) assembles `list(Xr, Yr, metadata)` shape consumed predict_ossl_mbl predict_ossl_plsr_local. result cached `tools::R_user_dir("soilKey", "cache")` subsequent calls session (future R sessions) skip network.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
code: download_ossl_subset(   region = c("global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia",     "oceania"),   properties = c("clay_pct", "sand_pct", "silt_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "ph_h2o",     "oc_pct", "fe_dcb_pct", "caco3_pct"),   wavelengths = 350:2500,   endpoint = NULL,   cache_dir = NULL,   force = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: region One "global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia", "oceania". Filters OSSL training rows site coordinates' continent. properties Character vector OSSL property names keep `Yr` (drops reference columns keep artefact small). Defaults WRB-relevant set used fill_from_spectra. wavelengths Integer vector wavelengths (nm) returned Xr matrix interpolated . Defaults Vis-NIR/SWIR (350-2500 nm 1-nm resolution, 2151 columns). endpoint OSSL HTTP endpoint serving JSON manifest; overrideable via options(soilKey.ossl_endpoint = ...) testing using private mirror. default public Soil Spectroscopy GG bucket. cache_dir Cache directory; defaults tools::R_user_dir("soilKey", "cache"). force TRUE, re-fetches even cached subset exists. verbose TRUE, emits `cli` summary fetch.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: list elements Xr (numeric matrix, rows =         training profiles, columns = wavelengths nm),         Yr (data.frame requested property columns,         rows aligned Xr), metadata (snapshot         date, region, n profiles, source URL, SHA-256         cache file). Pass ossl_library         argument fill_from_spectra         predict_ossl_mbl.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: function intentionally fall back synthetic predictor network failure – missing OSSL artefact real condition caller must handle, silent fallback make benchmarks meaningless.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: Sanderman, J., Savage, K., Dangal, S.R.S., Duran, G., Rivard, C., Cardona, M.T., Sandzhieva, ., Aramian, . & Safanelli, J.L. (2024). Soil Spectroscopy Global Good – Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL). https://soilspectroscopy.org/.

what: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: Fetches region-filtered slice Open Soil Spectral Library via download_ossl_subset post-joins WRB Reference Soil Group labels WoSIS GraphQL spatial nearest-neighbour. resulting artefact canonical list(Xr, Yr, metadata) shape – extra columns Yr: wrb_rsg, wrb_label_source, wrb_label_distance_km, plus optionally sibcs_ordem usda_order translate_systems = TRUE.

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
code: download_ossl_subset_with_labels(   region = c("global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia",     "oceania"),   max_distance_km = 5,   wosis_endpoint = NULL,   translate_systems = TRUE,   max_to_label = Inf,   verbose = TRUE,   query_fn = NULL,   ... )

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: region OSSL region filter; one "global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia", "oceania". max_distance_km WoSIS spatial-join tolerance kilometres (default 5). Profiles whose nearest WRB-labeled WoSIS neighbour farther left unlabeled. wosis_endpoint Override WoSIS GraphQL endpoint (default getOption("soilKey.wosis_graphql")). canonical value "https://graphql.isric.org/wosis/graphql". translate_systems TRUE (default), also adds sibcs_ordem usda_order columns derived WRB label via Schad (2023) Annex Table 1 / SiBCS 5ª ed. Annex correspondence. translations 1:N classes; pick -common partner tag rows translation genuinely ambiguous. max_to_label Maximum number profiles query WoSIS (default Inf). WoSIS throttles aggressive queries; cap running interactive demos. verbose Emit cli progress messages. query_fn Optional injection per-coordinate WoSIS query function. Default uses .query_nearest_wosis_wrb. Tests pass stub exercise join logic without network. ... Forwarded download_ossl_subset.

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: list Xr (numeric matrix), Yr (data         frame labels attached), metadata         (list OSSL fetch metadata + join statistics:         number profiles labeled, average / max distance,         WoSIS endpoint, snapshot date).

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: OSSL stores Vis-NIR / MIR spectra lab data typically lacks WRB Reference Soil Group labels profiles (KSSL data USDA-flavoured; non-US contributions inconsistent). WoSIS, contrast, archives ~228 000 profiles WRB labels spectra. function bridges two user can run classify_by_spectral_neighbours real-data OSSL library without spatial join .

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: WRB labels obtained via spatial join weak labels. physical location may classified differently across surveys (different WRB editions, different interpretations). row carries: wrb_label_source = "wosis_spatial_join": label         inherited WoSIS neighbour within         max_distance_km. wrb_label_distance_km: distance         neighbour (NA neighbour found within         tolerance). wrb_label_source = "ossl_native": label         already present OSSL Yr (rare; preserved verbatim). wrb_label_source = "missing": neighbour within         tolerance; row stays unlabeled skipped         downstream. Treat labels priors, ground truth.

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Real OSSL South-America subset with WRB labels: lib <- download_ossl_subset_with_labels(   region          = "south_america",   max_distance_km = 10 ) table(lib$Yr$wrb_rsg, useNA = "always") table(lib$Yr$wrb_label_source)  # Drop into the spectral analogy classifier: res <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum     = my_query_spectrum,   ossl_library = lib,   k            = 25,   region       = list(lat = -22.7, lon = -43.7,                       radius_km = 500) ) } # }

what: Extract horizons from a soil description PDF — extract_horizons_from_pdf
title: Extract horizons from a soil description PDF — extract_horizons_from_pdf
text: Reads PDF (typically soil survey chapter, field-sheet scan, thesis appendix), prompts configured VLM extract horizon attributes inst/schemas/horizon.json, merges result pedon. Every extracted attribute recorded source = "extracted_vlm" model's reported confidence verbatim source quote.

title: Extract horizons from a soil description PDF — extract_horizons_from_pdf
code: extract_horizons_from_pdf(   pedon,   pdf_path = NULL,   provider,   max_retries = 3L,   overwrite = FALSE,   prompt_name = "extract_horizons",   schema_name = "horizon",   pdf_text = NULL )

title: Extract horizons from a soil description PDF — extract_horizons_from_pdf
text: pedon PedonRecord merge . Mutated place returned invisibly. pdf_path Path PDF file. Either pdf_path pdf_text must supplied. provider chat provider vlm_provider (MockVLMProvider testing). max_retries Integer; many times re-prompt validation failure. Default 3. overwrite TRUE, lower-authority values allowed clobber higher-authority ones. Default FALSE. prompt_name Override default prompt template ("extract_horizons"). schema_name Override default schema ("horizon"). pdf_text Optional alternative pdf_path: already-extracted description text. Useful smoke tests, unit tests without pdftools, already-OCR'd field-sheet text.

title: Extract horizons from a soil description PDF — extract_horizons_from_pdf
text: Invisibly, (mutated) pedon. Carries         "vlm_extraction" attribute parsed response,         number attempts, number provenance entries added.

title: Extract horizons from a soil description PDF — extract_horizons_from_pdf
text: PedonRecord's authority order guarantees values already tagged "measured" never silently overwritten VLM extraction unless overwrite = TRUE. PDF long (~30,000 characters), chunked page--page page sent independently. conservative--simple strategy; long surveys callers pre-chunk call function per profile.

title: Extract horizons from a soil description PDF — extract_horizons_from_pdf
text: pdftools installed -> error. PDF read -> error. VLM response fails JSON parse / schema validation         max_retries + 1 attempts -> error         validate_or_retry.

title: Familia: constituicao esqueletica (Cap 1, p 48) — familia_constituicao_esqueletica
text: O schema atual nao distingue cascalho (2 mm-2 cm) de calhaus (> 2 cm). Como aproximacao conservadora, esta funcao retorna "esqueletica" quando coarse_fragments_pct esta intervalo (35%, 90%). Refinamento futuro requer adicionar uma coluna distinta para fragmentos > 2 cm.

title: Familia: constituicao esqueletica (Cap 1, p 48) — familia_constituicao_esqueletica
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 1, p 48; Cap 18,             p 284.

title: Familia: distribuicao de cascalhos no perfil (Cap 1, p 47-48) — familia_distribuicao_cascalhos
text: Utiliza coarse_fragments_pct (% volume de cascalhos 2 mm 2 cm relativo terra fina) como modificador grupamento textural.

Familia: distribuicao de cascalhos no perfil (Cap 1, p 47-48) — familia_distribuicao_cascalhos

text: Classes (Santos et al., 2015; valores em g kg-1): pouco_cascalhenta: 8% <= cascalho < 15% cascalhenta: 15% <= cascalho <= 50% muito_cascalhenta: cascalho > 50% Aplica-se TODAS classes que apresentam cascalho > 80 g/kg (8% volume) na secao de controle.

title: Familia: distribuicao de cascalhos no perfil (Cap 1, p 47-48) — familia_distribuicao_cascalhos

title: Familia: grupamento textural (Cap 1, p 46) — familia_grupamento_textural
text: Retorna o grupamento textural solo na secao de controle. Classes (em g kg-1): arenosa: areia + areia franca, .e. (sand_pct - clay_pct) > 70 media: clay < 35 e sand > 15, exceto arenosa argilosa: clay entre 35 e 60 muito_argilosa: clay > 60 siltosa: clay < 35 e sand < 15

Familia: grupamento textural (Cap 1, p 46) — familia_grupamento_textural
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade da secao de controle (default 200 cm).

text: Concatena os value nao-nulos como string separada por virgulas, conforme orientado Cap 18, p 281: "caracteristicas utilizadas para identificacao 5o nivel categorico devem ser acrescentadas apos designacao 4o nivel categorico e separadas desta e entre si por virgula".

what: Familia: mineralogia da fracao areia (Cap 18, p 286) — familia_mineralogia_areia
title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao areia (Cap 18, p 286) — familia_mineralogia_areia
text: Identifica predominio de minerais facilmente alteraveis na fracao areia (>= 0,05 mm) na secao de controle. Classes: micacea: sand_mica_pct >= 15 (% volume). anfibolitica: sand_amphibole_pct >= 15. feldspatica: sand_feldspar_pct >= 15.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao areia (Cap 18, p 286) — familia_mineralogia_areia
Familia: mineralogia da fracao areia (Cap 18, p 286) — familia_mineralogia_areia
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade da secao de controle (default 200). threshold Limiar de % volume (default 15).

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao areia (Cap 18, p 286) — familia_mineralogia_areia
text: FamilyAttribute.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao areia (Cap 18, p 286) — familia_mineralogia_areia
text: Quando os percentuais especificos estao ausentes, busca coluna sand_mineralogy (atalho qualitativo, valores aceitos: "micacea", "anfibolitica", "feldspatica"). Aplicavel Cambissolos, Chernossolos, Gleissolos, Luvissolos, Neossolos (excepto Quartzarenicos), Nitossolos, Planossolos, Plintossolos e Vertissolos.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao areia (Cap 18, p 286) — familia_mineralogia_areia
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 18, p 286.

what: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
text: Classifica mineralogia da argila para Argissolos, Cambissolos, Plintossolos, Luvissolos, Nitossolos, Vertissolos, Chernossolos, Planossolos, Gleissolos quando ha informacao quantitativa de atividade da argila e/ou Ki/Kr. Cobre classes nao endereçadas por familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo: esmectitica: T_argila >= ta_threshold (default         27 cmolc/kg argila), indicando dominancia de argilas 2:1         expansivas (esmectita / vermiculita / micas hidratadas). caulinitica: Ki >= ki_caulinitico_min (default         0.75) e Kr >= kr_caulinitico_min (default 0.75),         alem de T_argila < ta_threshold. oxidica: Kr < kr_caulinitico_min, indicando         predominancia de oxihidrooxidos de Fe e Al. mista: nenhum dos outros gates fechou         conclusivamente – evidencia heterogenea ou incompleta. Quando os tres atributos (T_argila, Ki, Kr) estiverem ausentes, o resultado fica NULL e os atributos faltantes sao reportados.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
code: familia_mineralogia_argila_geral(   pedon,   max_depth_cm = 200,   ta_threshold = 27,   ki_caulinitico_min = 0.75,   kr_caulinitico_min = 0.75 )

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade da secao de controle (default 200). ta_threshold Limite cmolc/kg argila para esmectitica (default 27). ki_caulinitico_min Limite Ki para caulinitica (default 0.75). kr_caulinitico_min Limite Kr para caulinitica vs oxidica (default 0.75).

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
text: FamilyAttribute.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 18, p 286-287.

what: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila para Latossolos (Cap 18, p 286-287) — familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo
title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila para Latossolos (Cap 18, p 286-287) — familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo
text: Classifica via Ki = SiO2/(Al2O3) e Kr = SiO2/(Al2O3 + Fe2O3) molares (helpers compute_ki / compute_kr): caulinitico: Ki > 0.75 e Kr > 0.75 caulinitico-oxidico: Ki > 0.75 e Kr <= 0.75 gibsitico-oxidico: Ki <= 0.75 e Kr <= 0.75 oxidico: Kr <= 0.75 (predominio Fe2O3 + Al2O3)

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila para Latossolos (Cap 18, p 286-287) — familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo
Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila para Latossolos (Cap 18, p 286-287) — familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade da secao de controle (default 200).

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila para Latossolos (Cap 18, p 286-287) — familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo
text: FamilyAttribute.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila para Latossolos (Cap 18, p 286-287) — familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo
text: Aplicavel principalmente para Latossolos; tambem pode ser usado em Argissolos, Cambissolos e Plintossolos quando ha informacao de mineralogia da argila pelo menos semiquantitativa.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila para Latossolos (Cap 18, p 286-287) — familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 18, p 286-287.

text: Classifica presenca de galhos / fragmentos de troncos > 2 cm em camadas organicas, "semelhanca utilizado para qualificar classes de pedregosidade" (Cap 18 p 288): lenhoso: 10% <= woody_fragments < 30% muito_lenhoso: 30% <= woody_fragments <= 50% extremamente_lenhoso: woody_fragments > 50% (Limites adotados partir das classes de pedregosidade, Santos et al. 2015.)

what: Familia: teor de oxidos de ferro (Cap 1, p 42) — familia_oxidos_ferro
title: Familia: teor de oxidos de ferro (Cap 1, p 42) — familia_oxidos_ferro
text: Classifica pelo teor de Fe2O3 (g/kg de solo, equivalente fe2o3_sulfuric_pct * 10) na maior parte horizonte B: hipoferrico: < 80 g/kg (= < 8%) mesoferrico: 80 - 180 g/kg ([8%, 18%)) ferrico: 180 - 360 g/kg ([18%, 36%)) perferrico: >= 360 g/kg (>= 36%)

text: Aplicavel Argissolos, Cambissolos, Chernossolos, Latossolos, Neossolos Litolicos, Neossolos Regoliticos, Nitossolos e Plintossolos. Quando o atributo ja foi considerado em nivel categorico mais alto (e.g. Latossolos Eutroferricos / Distroferricos / Acriferricos), o motor de Familia pula esta dimensao.

title: Familia: prefixo de profundidade epi-/meso-/endo- (Cap 18, p 284-285) — familia_prefixo_profundidade
text: Classifica profundidade onde um diagnostico ocorre em um dos tres prefixos: epi-: topo da primeira camada que satisfaz < 50 cm meso-: topo da primeira camada em [50, 100) cm endo-: topo da primeira camada em >= 100 cm

text: String "epi" / "meso" / "endo" ou NULL.

title: Familia: prefixo de profundidade epi-/meso-/endo- (Cap 18, p 284-285) — familia_prefixo_profundidade
text: Wrapper generico para ser usado com qualquer DiagnosticResult. Retorna NULL se o diagnostico nao passou ou se nao ha camadas identificadas.

title: Familia: prefixo de profundidade epi-/meso-/endo- (Cap 18, p 284-285) — familia_prefixo_profundidade

title: Familia: saturacao por aluminio –
text: Aplica o termo "alico" quando, em qualquer camada horizonte B (ou C, na ausencia de B): al_sat_pct >= 50% (saturacao por Al = 100*Al/(S+Al)), E al_cmol > 0.5 cmolc/kg. Quando aplicavel, o prefixo de profundidade (epi-/meso-/endo-) eh determinado pelo topo da primeira camada que satisfaz, e concatenado ao adjetivo: "epialico", "mesoalico", "endoalico".

title: Familia: saturacao por aluminio –
Familia: saturacao por aluminio –
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_al_sat Default 50. min_al_cmol Default 0.5.

title: Familia: saturacao por aluminio –
text: FamilyAttribute com value igual         "epialico" / "mesoalico" / "endoalico"         ou NULL.

title: Familia: saturacao por aluminio –
text: Aplicavel classes cujo carater alumınico nao tenha sido considerado em nivel categorico mais alto (p.ex. Argissolos Aluminicos ja o usam).

title: Familia: saturacao por aluminio –
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 18, p 285.

what: Familia: subgrupamento textural (Cap 18, p 283; em validacao) — familia_subgrupamento_textural
title: Familia: subgrupamento textural (Cap 18, p 283; em validacao) — familia_subgrupamento_textural
text: Subgrupamento textural mais detalhado, aplicavel em substituicao ao grupamento para Espodossolos, Latossolos psamiticos, Neossolos Fluvicos Psamiticos, Neossolos Regoliticos, Neossolos Quartzarenicos, e SGs arenicos / espessarenicos de Argissolos / Luvissolos / Planossolos / Plintossolos. Tambem em solos com textura arenosa e/ou media.

title: Familia: subgrupamento textural (Cap 18, p 283; em validacao) — familia_subgrupamento_textural
Familia: subgrupamento textural (Cap 18, p 283; em validacao) — familia_subgrupamento_textural
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade da secao de controle (default 200 cm).

text: FamilyAttribute.

title: Familia: subgrupamento textural (Cap 18, p 283; em validacao) — familia_subgrupamento_textural
text: Classes (em g kg-1; referidas media ponderada da secao de controle): muito_arenosa: classe textural areia (sand >= 85) arenosa-media: classe textural areia franca (sand >= 70 e     <= 91; clay <= 15) media-arenosa: francoarenosa, sand > 52 media-argilosa: franco-argiloarenosa (clay 20-35, sand >=     45) media-siltosa: clay < 35 e sand > 15, excluindo 4     classes acima siltosa: clay < 35 e sand < 15 argilosa: clay 35-60 muito_argilosa: clay > 60

title: Familia: subgrupamento textural (Cap 18, p 283; em validacao) — familia_subgrupamento_textural
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 18, p. 283.

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: Tests whether horizon meets ferralic horizon criteria. ferralic horizon subsurface horizon resulting long intense weathering, characterized low cation exchange capacity per unit clay – canonical "low-activity clay" signal defines Ferralsol RSG.

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 30). max_cec Maximum CEC (1M NH4OAc, pH 7) per kg clay (default 16).

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: Sub-tests called: test_ferralic_texture – texture sandy loam         finer. test_cec_per_clay – CEC / clay <= 16         cmol_c/kg clay. test_ferralic_thickness – thickness >= 30 cm. v0.3.1 alignment WRB 2022 Ch 3.1.10 (p. 44): older "ECEC <= 12 cmol_c/kg clay" gate removed canonical text – CEC (1M NH4OAc, pH 7) <= 16 required. weatherable-mineral test (<= 10% volume), water-dispersible-clay test, stratification / rock-structure exclusions remain deferred (need mineralogical data outside canonical horizon schema) refinements rather gates.

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3.1.10 – Ferralic horizon (p. 44).

what: Ferralsol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 110) — ferralsol
title: Ferralsol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 110) — ferralsol
text: WRB-canonical: ferralic horizon \<= 150 cm argic horizon starting (upper limit ) ferralic, UNLESS argic upper 30 cm throughout one : < 10% water-dispersible clay; DeltapH (pH_KCl - pH_water) \>= 0; \>= 1.4% soil organic carbon. v0.3.4 enforces three exception paths. DeltapH check uses ph_kcl ph_h2o; WDC check uses water_dispersible_clay_pct (introduced v0.3.3 schema).

title: Ferralsol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 110) — ferralsol

what: Fill missing soil attributes from spectra via OSSL — fill_from_spectra
title: Fill missing soil attributes from spectra via OSSL — fill_from_spectra
text: Given PedonRecord carrying spectra$vnir matrix (rows = horizons, columns = wavelengths nm), pre-processes spectra, predicts requested soil properties using chosen OSSL-backed method, writes predictions pedon's horizons table via pedon$add_measurement(..., source = "predicted_spectra"). call updates pedon's provenance log downstream classification can derive evidence grade.

title: Fill missing soil attributes from spectra via OSSL — fill_from_spectra
code: fill_from_spectra(   pedon,   library = "ossl",   region = c("global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa"),   properties = c("clay_pct", "sand_pct", "silt_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "ph_h2o",     "oc_pct", "fe_dcb_pct", "caco3_pct"),   method = c("mbl", "plsr_local", "pretrained"),   preprocess = "snv+sg1",   k_neighbors = 100L,   overwrite = FALSE,   ossl_library = NULL,   ossl_models = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fill missing soil attributes from spectra via OSSL — fill_from_spectra
text: pedon PedonRecord spectra$vnir matrix. library Currently "ossl" supported. region One "global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa". Used subset OSSL training data supported underlying backend. properties Character vector OSSL-supported property names predict. Default covers -requested WRB/SiBCS-relevant attributes. method One "mbl", "plsr_local", "pretrained". preprocess Pre-processing pipeline; passed preprocess_spectra. k_neighbors Number neighbours memory-based methods. overwrite FALSE (default), fill cells whose existing provenance weaker predicted_spectra. ossl_library Optional OSSL library object (see predict_ossl_mbl). ossl_models Optional named list pretrained models (see predict_ossl_pretrained). verbose TRUE, prints cli summary.

title: Fill missing soil attributes from spectra via OSSL — fill_from_spectra

title: Histic epipedon (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — histic_epipedon_usda
text: KST 13ed required characteristics (Ch. 3, pp 13-15): Saturated 30+ days/year (artificially drained); Organic soil material either: 20-60 cm thick (Sphagnum >= 75 percent             bulk_density < 0.1 g/cm3); 20-40 cm thick (general); Ap horizon mixed 25 cm OC >= 8 percent weight. Implementation notes (v0.8.x): Saturation detected via horizon designation         starting H (per KST notation) via WRB         horizonte_glei fallback redoximorphic         features present. Sphagnum content uses WRB fiber_content_rubbed_pct         column (>= 75 means fibrous); refinement true         Sphagnum-specific column deferred.

title: Histic epipedon (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — histic_epipedon_usda
text: Soil Survey Staff (2022). Keys Soil Taxonomy,   13th edition, USDA-NRCS, Washington DC. Ch. 3, pp. 13-15.

title: Histic horizon (WRB 2022) — histic_horizon
text: surface (near-surface, drainage) horizon organic material; diagnostic Histosols. Two alternative qualifying paths per WRB 2022: Contiguous: single layer organic material         (OC % >= min_oc) reaching surface         least min_thickness cm thick (default 10 cm). Cumulative: organic material totalling         cumulative_min_cm cm (default 40) within upper         cumulative_max_depth_cm (default 80). Relevant         folic / mossy Histosols slopes. Either path qualifies. "drainage" qualifier (recently drained organic soils) treated implicit since OC thickness criteria apply.

title: Histic horizon (WRB 2022) — histic_horizon
code: histic_horizon(   pedon,   min_thickness = 10,   min_oc = 12,   surface_top_cm = 0,   cumulative_min_cm = 40,   cumulative_max_depth_cm = 80 )

title: Histic horizon (WRB 2022) — histic_horizon
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness (cm) contiguous path (default 10). min_oc Minimum organic carbon % (default 12, WRB 2022; equivalent >= 20% organic matter). surface_top_cm Maximum top depth (cm) layer considered "surface-related" contiguous path (default 0). cumulative_min_cm Minimum cumulative thickness (cm) cumulative path (default 40). cumulative_max_depth_cm Depth window (cm) cumulative path (default 80).

title: Leptic features (WRB 2022) — leptic_features
text: Tests whether continuous rock rock-like material occurs within max_depth cm surface. Two alternative paths qualify per WRB 2022: Designation: layer depth <= max_depth         designation matching "^R" "^Cr"         (continuous rock weathered rock-like substrate). Coarse fragments: layer depth <= max_depth         coarse_fragments_pct >= min_coarse_pct (default         90% volume), interpreted rock-dominated even         R / Cr-designated. Either path qualifies.

title: Melanic epipedon (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — melanic_epipedon_usda
text: KST 13ed required characteristics (Ch. 3, pp 15-16): Upper boundary within 30 cm mineral soil         surface (organic layer andic properties); Cumulative thickness >= 30 cm within 40 cm : Andic soil properties throughout; Color value <= 2.5 moist chroma <= 2 throughout; Melanic index <= 1.70 (deferred – specialized lab             measurement); OC >= 6 percent (weighted) >= 4 percent (            layer). Implementation notes (v0.8.x): Andic soil properties tested via andic_properties_usda         (v0.9; v0.8 approximate bulk_density <=         0.9 g/cm3 phosphate_retention >= 85%). Melanic index (= 100 / (OC * 100 + 1) per KST appendix)         deferred – requires UV-Vis spectroscopy.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: Tests whether near-surface horizon meets mollic horizon criteria. mollic horizon diagnostic surface horizon Chernozems, Phaeozems, Kastanozems, several RSGs; indicates thick, dark, base-rich, organic-matter-enriched topsoil formed steppe comparable vegetation.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
code: mollic(   pedon,   min_thickness = 20,   min_oc = 0.6,   min_bs = 50,   surface_top_cm = 5 )

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 20). min_oc Minimum SOC % (default 0.6). min_bs Minimum base saturation % (default 50). surface_top_cm Maximum top depth (cm) horizon considered "surface-related" (default 5). v0.1 uses proxy WRB rule mollic must form continuously soil surface (mixing upper 20 cm required).

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: Sub-tests called: test_mollic_color – moist value <= 3, moist         chroma <= 3, dry value <= 5. test_mollic_organic_carbon – SOC >= 0.6%. test_mollic_base_saturation – BS (NH4OAc, pH 7)         >= 50%. test_mollic_thickness – horizon thickness >= 20 cm. test_mollic_structure – simultaneously         massive hard dry. v0.1 limitations: cumulative thickness across contiguous mollic- qualifying horizons yet supported – matters profiles mollic criteria met A1+A2 sequence single horizon >= 20 cm thick. Mixing upper 20 cm test (per WRB) also deferred v0.2.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3 – Mollic horizon.

title: Mollic epipedon (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — mollic_epipedon_usda
text: KST 13ed required characteristics (Ch. 3, pp 15-17): Color: dominant color value <= 3 (moist) <= 5 (dry)         chroma <= 3 (moist), adjustments CaCO3         content (deferred v0.9); Base saturation (NH4OAc, pH 7) >= 50 percent throughout; Organic carbon >= 0.6 percent (2.5 percent value         4-5 moist; 0.6 absolute > C horizon); Thickness: 18 cm general, 25 cm texture loamy fine         sand coarser, 10 cm directly lithic/densic/         paralithic contact (thin_lithic_overlay branch); Structure: peds <= 30 cm rupture-resistance <=         moderately hard; part moist 90+ days soil temp 50 cm         >= 5 C (deferred – requires climatic data). Implementation notes (v0.8.x): Thickness rule computed dynamically based texture         presence underlying lithic/paralithic contact. N value < 0.7 / fluidity nonfluid assumed (laboratory         tests rarely available); 90-day moisture condition deferred v0.9.

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: Tests nitic horizon: clay-rich (>= 30%), Fe-rich (DCB Fe >= 4%) subsurface horizon least 30 cm thick. Diagnostic Nitisols. WRB 2022 additionally requires polyhedral / nutty structure shiny ped surfaces gradual (non-abrupt) clay decrease depth.

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
code: nitic_horizon(   pedon,   min_clay = 30,   min_fe_dcb = 4,   min_thickness = 30,   max_clay_drop_pct = 8,   max_decrease_depth = 50 )

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_clay Minimum clay % (default 30). min_fe_dcb Minimum DCB-extractable Fe % (default 4). min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 30). max_clay_drop_pct Maximum clay drop (percentage points) adjacent layers within max_decrease_depth failing gradual-decrease test (default 8). max_decrease_depth Depth window (cm) gradual-decrease check (default 50).

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: Required (-combined) sub-tests: Profile ferralic horizon (Ferralsol path         canonical clay-rich + low-CEC corner). clay % >= min_clay. fe_dcb_pct >= min_fe_dcb. thickness >= min_thickness. Supplementary (soft-) sub-tests – evaluated evidence present pedon, evaluate NA (fail) missing: structure_type matches polyhedral / nutty / (sub)angular         blocky. slickensides / shiny ped surfaces present (proxy         WRB's "shiny ped surfaces"). clay decrease abruptly adjacent layers         within 50 cm surface (gradual-decrease pattern;         drop > 8 percentage points fails). Supplementary tests fail (return passed = FALSE) evidence actively contradicts criterion; missing evidence permissive.

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3, Nitic horizon.

text: v0.9.2.C tightening: v0.3.3 implementation accepted thick, low-BD, OC-rich horizon, -fired across natural mollic / umbric / chernic surfaces. diagnostic now requires, addition OC + BD + thickness baseline, least one independent anthropogenic-input marker: p_mehlich3_mg_kg >= 50 (sustained sod / manure         additions concentrate Mehlich-3 P topsoil), artefacts_pct > 0 (human artefact volume fraction         sufficient presence signal), designation pattern Apl / Aplg / Apk         / explicit "plagg". Without one markers diagnostic returns FALSE even OC + BD + thickness pass. mirrors v0.9.1 qual_plaggic gate enforces rule diagnostic level caller (SiBCS, USDA, future modules) inherits protection.

what: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: Predicts set soil properties pre-processed Vis-NIR MIR spectra using memory-based learning (MBL) – recommended OSSL workflow heterogeneous libraries. Defaults follow literature (Ramirez-Lopez et al., 2013): k = 100 neighbours, PLS-score dissimilarity, local PLS regression 5 components, internal leave-one-validation.

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
code: predict_ossl_mbl(   X,   properties,   region = "global",   k = 100L,   ossl_library = NULL,   ... )

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: X pre-processed numeric matrix (rows = horizons, columns = wavelengths). properties Character vector OSSL-supported property names. region One "global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa". k Integer number neighbours. ossl_library Optional list OSSL training spectra (Xr) reference values (Yr, data.frame keyed properties). NULL, synthetic path used. ... Additional arguments forwarded resemble::mbl.

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: data.table columns horizon_idx, property,         value, pi95_low, pi95_high, n_neighbors.         "backend" attribute records path taken         ("resemble" "synthetic").

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: resemble::mbl installed ossl_library artefact supplied (list elements Xr, Yr) function delegates resemble::mbl(); otherwise returns deterministic synthetic prediction conditioned input spectra downstream code, tests vignettes run without external dependencies. fallback annotated via notes attribute returned data.table.

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: Ramirez-Lopez, L., Behrens, T., Schmidt, K., Stevens, ., Demattê, J. . M., & Scholten, T. (2013). spectrum-based learner: new local approach modeling soil Vis-NIR spectra complex datasets. Geoderma, 195–196, 268–279.

what: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: Applies chosen pre-processing pipeline numeric matrix soil spectra. Rows samples (typically horizons) columns wavelengths. Returns numeric matrix; SG-based methods shorten spectrum w - 1 columns edges (default w = 5 two columns dropped side).

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: X Numeric matrix data.frame spectra (rows = samples, columns = wavelengths). Wavelengths evenly spaced. method One "snv", "sg1", "snv+sg1". Default "snv+sg1". w Window size SG filter. Must odd; default 5. p Polynomial order SG filter. Default 2.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: numeric matrix. Column names (wavelengths) preserved         possible; SG trimming drops (w - 1) / 2         columns edge.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: Supported method values: "snv" Standard Normal Variate. row centered         mean divided standard deviation. "sg1" Savitzky-Golay 1st derivative window         five wavelengths quadratic polynomial. "snv+sg1" SNV followed SG1 (default; standard         pipeline used OSSL pretrained models Vis-NIR). prospectr available, use prospectr::standardNormalVariate prospectr::savitzkyGolay (Rcpp implementation, faster supports arbitrary window/polynomial). native fallback uses classical 5-point first-derivative coefficients (-2, -1, 0, 1, 2) / 10, closed-form Savitzky-Golay solution window 5 / polynomial 2 / derivative 1.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: Savitzky, ., & Golay, M. J. E. (1964). Smoothing differentiation data simplified least squares procedures. Analytical Chemistry, 36(8), 1627–1639. Barnes, R. J., Dhanoa, M. S., & Lister, S. J. (1989). Standard Normal Variate transformation de-trending near-infrared diffuse reflectance spectra. Applied Spectroscopy, 43(5), 772–777. Stevens, ., & Ramirez-Lopez, L. (2024). prospectr: Misc. functions processing sample selection spectroscopic data. R package version 0.2.7.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
code: set.seed(1) X <- matrix(runif(5 * 2151, 0, 1), nrow = 5) colnames(X) <- 350:2500 Xp <- preprocess_spectra(X, method = "snv+sg1") dim(Xp)  # 5 x 2147 (4 columns dropped by SG window 5) #> [1]    5 2147

title: Authority order for provenance sources — provenance_authority

what: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: Writes single GeoPackage (.gpkg) QGIS reads natively, containing one POINT layer (profile location classification metadata attributes) plus two attribute-tables (horizons schema provenance log). Lets pedologist overlay soilKey result soil-survey base map join field-campaign vector data without writing R SQL.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
code: report_to_qgis(   pedon,   classifications,   file,   report_html = NULL,   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: pedon PedonRecord. classifications list one three ClassificationResult objects, named wrb / sibcs / usda. Pass output classify_from_documents verbatim, build list manually. file Output path (.gpkg). Created parents. report_html Optional path sibling HTML report (rendered via report_html) – stored report_html attribute pedon_point QGIS users can launch report feature pop-. overwrite TRUE (default), existing file replaced; otherwise error thrown.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: output file path, invisibly. Side-effect:         writes multi-layer GeoPackage.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: point geometry uses pedon's site CRS (pedon$site$crs, default EPSG:4326). site coordinates, function still writes two attribute tables skips point layer emits warning.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis

code: salic(   pedon,   min_thickness = 15,   min_ec_dS_m = 15,   alkaline_min_ec_dS_m = 8,   alkaline_min_pH = 8.5,   min_product = 450,   alkaline_min_product = 240 )

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 15). min_ec_dS_m Primary EC threshold (default 15 dS/m 25C). alkaline_min_ec_dS_m Alkaline-path EC threshold (default 8 dS/m, used pH(H2O) \>= alkaline_min_pH). alkaline_min_pH Required pH(H2O) alkaline path (default 8.5). min_product Primary path product (EC * thickness dS/m * cm) threshold (default 450 per WRB 2022). alkaline_min_product Alkaline-path product threshold (default 240).

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: Sub-tests called: test_ec_concentration – EC \>= 15 dS/m         (primary) (EC \>= 8 dS/m pH(H2O) \>= 8.5)         (alkaline). test_minimum_thickness – thickness \>= 15 cm. test_salic_product – EC * thickness product         \>= 450 (primary) \>= 240 (alkaline) per qualifying         layer. v0.3.1: alkaline-path product test added (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1.20, p. 49). Earlier versions enforced primary EC + thickness gate.

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3.1.20 – Salic horizon (p. 49).

what: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: soilKey implements deterministic classification keys World Reference Base Soil Resources 2022 (4th edition) Brazilian System Soil Classification (SiBCS, 5th edition). separates concerns strictly: taxonomic key pure function structured profile data, optional modules provide vision-language extraction, spatial priors SoilGrids, gap-filling soil attributes Vis-NIR MIR spectra via Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL).

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: never delegate key. Vision-language models restricted schema-validated extraction soil attributes unstructured sources (PDFs, photos, field sheets). taxonomic key always evaluated deterministic R code driven versioned YAML rules.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: PedonRecord — site, horizons, spectra, images,         documents, per-attribute provenance log. DiagnosticResult — return type every diagnostic         function (e.g. argic, ferralic,         mollic); always carries sub-test evidence         missing-attribute report alongside boolean. ClassificationResult — return type         classify_wrb2022; carries full key trace,         ambiguities, missing-data hints, provenance-aware evidence         grade.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: Every attribute used key carries provenance tag c("measured", "extracted_vlm", "predicted_spectra", "inferred_prior", "user_assumed"). final classification evidence grade one c("", "B", "C", "D") fully laboratory-measured unambiguous D tentative multimodal.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: v0.1 implements three WRB 2022 horizon diagnostics — argic, ferralic, mollic — Ferralsols path WRB key end--end. full 32-RSG key, 202 qualifiers, SiBCS key, multimodal extraction, spatial-prior, OSSL-spectroscopy modules scheduled subsequent releases. See ARCHITECTURE.md.

text: Sub-tests: test_spodic_aluminum_iron – (Al_ox +         0.5*Fe_ox) >= 0.5% test_ph_below – ph_h2o <= 5.9 test_minimum_thickness – thickness >= 2.5 cm v0.2 limitations: WRB color criterion (hue 5YR yellower chroma <= 5, specific dark colors) enforced. (Al_ox + Fe_ox)/clay >= 0.05 alternative ratio test yet wired. deferred v0.3.

Technic hard material (concrete, asphalt, mine spoil)         technic_hardmaterial_pct >= hardmaterial_min_pct         (default 95%) surface (top_cm <=         hardmaterial_max_top_cm, default 5). Either path qualifies.

what: Test the argic clay-increase criterion (WRB 2022) — test_clay_increase_argic
title: Test the argic clay-increase criterion (WRB 2022) — test_clay_increase_argic
text: Tests every consecutive pair horizons profile WRB 2022 (4th ed., Chapter 3.1.3, criterion 2..iv-vi) clay-increase rules. v0.3.1 brings compliance actual text (earlier versions used loosened thresholds 3/1.2/8 older WRB editions): overlying clay < 15%: argic horizon must contain least         **6% (absolute)** clay; overlying clay 15 <50%: argic / overlying clay ratio         must **>= 1.4**; overlying clay >= 50%: argic must contain least         **20% (absolute)** clay. Returns indices horizons satisfy argic candidates.

title: Umbric epipedon (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — umbric_epipedon_usda
text: KST 13ed required characteristics (Ch. 3, pp 18-20): Color: mollic (V<=3 moist, V<=5 dry, chroma<=3); Base saturation (NH4OAc) < 50 percent part; Organic carbon >= 0.6 percent (0.6 absolute > C); Thickness: rules mollic (18 / 25 / 10 cm); Structure: peds <= 30 cm rupture-resistance <= moderately         hard.

title: Vertic horizon (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1) — vertic_horizon
text: Stricter vertic *properties*: vertic *horizon* requires \>= 30% clay throughout, slickensides \>= "common" level, shrink-swell cracks \>= 0.5 cm wide. Used Vertisols. v0.9.19 adds -alternative COLE-based linear-extensibility path: summed (cole_value * thickness) upper 100 cm \>= 6 cm passes diagnostic even slickensides + cracks recorded (KST 13ed Ch 16 LE alternative, p 343).

title: Vertic horizon (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1) — vertic_horizon
code: vertic_horizon(   pedon,   min_clay = 30,   min_thickness = 25,   min_le_cm = 6,   le_max_depth_cm = 100 )

title: Vertic horizon (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1) — vertic_horizon
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_clay Numeric threshold option (see Details). min_thickness Numeric threshold option (see Details). min_le_cm Minimum LE sum (cm) COLE-based path (default 6, per KST 13ed Ch 16). le_max_depth_cm Depth window (cm) COLE-based path (default 100).

title: Vertic properties (WRB 2022) — vertic_properties

title: Vertic properties (WRB 2022) — vertic_properties
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_clay Minimum clay percent (default 30, per WRB 2022). min_thickness Minimum thickness (cm) vertic layer (default 25 per WRB 2022 Ch 3.2.x). slickenside_levels Vector slickensides values accepted evidence (default c("common", "many", "continuous")).

title: Vertic properties (WRB 2022) — vertic_properties
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Vertic properties (WRB 2022) — vertic_properties
text: Sub-tests: test_clay_above – clay >= 30% test_slickensides_present – slickensides         "common" level test_minimum_thickness – combined vertic layer         thickness >= 25 cm (v0.3.1 added per WRB 2022) v0.3.1: thickness gate added. Limitations remaining: WRB also accepts deep cracks (>= 1 cm wide extending surface >= 50 cm depth, soil dry) wedge-shaped peds alternative evidence; implementation requires clay + slickensides. "mixing upper 18 cm" clause WRB still deferred.

title: Vertic properties (WRB 2022) — vertic_properties
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3.2 – Vertic   properties.

Construct a VLM provider chat object — vlm_provider
text: name Provider name. One "anthropic" (Claude), "openai" (GPT-4o family), "google" (Gemini), "ollama" (local). model Optional model identifier; defaults default_model(name). ... Additional arguments forwarded corresponding ellmer::chat_* constructor (e.g. system_prompt, api_key, base_url, params).

title: Construct a VLM provider chat object — vlm_provider
text: ellmer Chat object exposing $chat()         method sending prompts.

title: Construct a VLM provider chat object — vlm_provider
text: purely convenience wrapper: picks default model per provider forwards remaining arguments (e.g. system_prompt, api_key) underlying ellmer constructor. ellmer must installed.

title: Construct a VLM provider chat object — vlm_provider
text: Passing name = "ollama" runs every extraction locally via Ollama server (default gemma4:e4b, Gemma 4 edge multimodal text+image+audio support). data leaves machine, recommended setting sensitive field descriptions (e.g. governmental surveys, indigenous land studies) institutional independence data sovereignty matter. Pull model first: start Ollama server (ollama serve) chat object returned dispatch HTTP locally.
code: ollama pull gemma4:e4b      # ~3 GB edge variant (default)   ollama pull gemma4:31b      # frontier dense variant   ollama pull gemma3:27b      # earlier generation, still solid

title: Construct a VLM provider chat object — vlm_provider
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Cloud provider (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) provider <- vlm_provider("anthropic")  # Local Gemma 4 edge model -- default, ~3 GB, runs anywhere provider <- vlm_provider("ollama")  # Local Gemma 4 frontier dense model -- best quality provider <- vlm_provider("ollama", model = "gemma4:31b")  # Any other multimodal model the user has pulled provider <- vlm_provider("ollama", model = "qwen2.5vl:32b") } # }

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: “field morphology unlocks lab” release. Integrates NASIS Morphological export (NASIS_Morphological_09142021.sqlite, 562 MB, 431 415 phorizon rows) existing NCSS Lab Data Mart GeoPackage. lab gpkg chemistry + physics; NASIS sqlite Munsell colour, structure grade, clay films, slickensides, cracks, surveyor-identified diagnostic horizons. Joining peiid (Pedon Element ID) unlocks every diagnostic gate needed field morphology fire.

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: Reads lab gpkg via existing load_kssl_pedons_gpkg(), joins pedon’s lab horizons matching NASIS phorizon (peiid, hzdept, hzdepb), pulls canonical horizon schema: phcolor -> munsell_hue_moist / munsell_value_moist / munsell_chroma_moist / munsell_*_dry (528 421 rows) phstructure -> structure_grade / structure_size / structure_type (lowercase-normalised; 421 881 rows) phpvsf (clay films) -> clay_films_amount (mapped pvsfpct soilKey’s qualitative tiers; 109 793 clay-film rows) phpvsf (slickensides pedogenic / non-intersecting) -> slickensides (4 275 rows) phcracks -> cracks_width_cm / cracks_depth_cm (170 rows) pediagfeatures -> site$nasis_diagnostic_features (64 169 rows – surveyor-identified diagnostic horizons; informational per-site list, currently fed deterministic key) matching depth-overlap-based: lab layer, find NASIS phorizon largest (hzdept, hzdepb) overlap. NASIS also provides richer designations (hzname) – lab gpkg designation NA, NASIS one used.

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: lab gpkg lacks every field morphology variable KST 13ed Ch 3 lists “diagnostic features disambiguate Order membership chemistry alone ambiguous”: Mollic epipedon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 15): requires Munsell value moist <= 3 + chroma <= 3. Lab gpkg zero Munsell. Argillic horizon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4): requires “evidence clay illuviation” (clay films, lamellae, oriented clay bridges). Lab gpkg clay percentages. Cambic horizon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 13): requires structure designation evidence weathering. Lab gpkg chemistry. Vertic horizon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 23): requires slickensides cracks LE >= 6 cm. Lab gpkg COLE. NASIS provides four: 99 % pedons least one Munsell record, 93 % structure data, 36 % clay films, 3 % slickensides directly recorded (another ~5 % via pediagfeatures.featkind = "Slickensides").

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: KST 13ed Ch 3 (spodic horizon, p 23) defines spodic horizon via several equivalent paths: (Al + 0.5*Fe)_ox >= 0.5 one; spodic morphology characteristic Bh / Bs designation + albic E + low pH + elevated B-horizon OC another (specific “field-described spodic” without lab Al / Fe). al_ox_pct fe_ox_pct missing across candidate layers, v0.9.19 falls back morphological path: designation matches ^Bh|^Bs|^Bhs|^Bsh, albic E horizon lies directly , pH(H2O) <= 5.9 Bh / Bs, OC Bh / Bs >= 0.5 % (illuvial accumulation evidence). fallback fires al_ox / fe_ox entirely absent pedon – lab-grade KSSL pedons still gate canonical chemical criteria.

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: KST 13ed Ch 16 (Vertisols, p 343) accepts linear extensibility (LE) summed upper 100 cm >= 6 cm alternative slickensides + cracks. v0.9.19 implements LE path: Triggers cole_value measured layer; uses canonical slickensides + cracks path cole_value absent.
code: LE = sum(cole_value[i] * thickness_cm[i])      for layers with top_cm < 100

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)

title: soilKey 0.9.15 (2026-04-30)
text: seven previously-simplified diagnostics now offers WRB 2022 alternative qualifying paths verbatim. -alternative aggregation via new aggregate_alternatives() helper. path’s evidence fully recorded DiagnosticResult$evidence trace stays inspectable. histic_horizon – adds cumulative path (>= 40 cm organic material within upper 80 cm), catching folic / mossy Histosols slopes contiguous-10cm path misses. anthric_horizons – adds property-based path (top_cm <= 5 + thickness >= 20 + Munsell value <= 4 + P-Mehlich >= 50), surveys describe properties (hortic/pretic/… designation) still qualify. technic_features – adds two new alternative paths: continuous geomembrane within 100 cm, technic hard material (concrete, asphalt, mine spoil) >= 95% within upper 5 cm. Adds geomembrane_present technic_hardmaterial_pct fields canonical horizon schema. cryic_conditions – adds explicit permafrost-temperature path (permafrost_temp_C <= 0 C within 100 cm), longer depending ^Cf / -f designation pattern alone. leptic_features – adds coarse-fragments path (coarse_fragments_pct >= 90 within 25 cm), rock-dominated profiles never formally R/Cr-designated still qualify. andic_properties – adds WRB 2022 phosphate-retention alternative (phosphate_retention_pct >= 70). volcanic-glass alternative remains separate vitric_properties() diagnostic; Andosol RSG gate (andosol()) keys (andic vitric). nitic_horizon – adds three supplementary tests -combined primary clay/Fe/thickness gate: polyhedral / nutty structure_type, gradual clay decrease depth (>8 pp drop upper 50 cm), shiny-ped-surface evidence (recorded evidence , gating, since schema lacks dedicated field). Tests permissive missing data; conclusively-FALSE evidence forces diagnostic fail.

text: Replaced dead Embrapa URL (geoinfo.cnps.embrapa.br) current Embrapa Solos / SiBCS landing page (---cran invalid-URL NOTE). pkgdown workflow: _pkgdown.yml now references ossl_demo_sa (topic failed pkgdown CI v0.9.11 shipped data/). test-coverage workflow: fail_ci_if_error: false codecov-action step (badge informational; tokenless uploads protected branches need CODECOV_TOKEN secret succeed – without , CI used go red). GitHub Pages source switched main branch (Jekyll chokes .Rmd vignettes) gh-pages branch (pkgdown workflow already pushes built site .nojekyll).

title: soilKey 0.9.11 (2026-04-30)
text: Post-release pass triggered v0.9.10 Zenodo DOI minting (10.5281/zenodo.19930112 concept-DOI). Three substantive additions: real Gemma 4 support, high-level classify_from_documents() one-liner, first empirical run real WoSIS data via GraphQL.

title: soilKey 0.9.11 (2026-04-30)
text: classify_from_documents(pdf, image, fieldsheet, provider, ...) – high-level one-liner promised ARCHITECTURE.md § 10: takes soil-description PDF / profile-wall image, extracts horizons + Munsell + site metadata via configured VLM provider (default: local Gemma 4 edge), runs three keys (WRB / SiBCS / USDA), optionally writes self-contained HTML / PDF report. architectural invariants preserved: VLM never classifies, every extracted value carries source = "extracted_vlm", evidence_grade reflects provenance. Gemma 4 default Ollama. default model vlm_provider("ollama") now gemma4:e4b (Gemma 4 edge, ~3 GB, multimodal text+image+audio). Gemma 4 released Google DeepMind 2026; ships five sizes (E2B / E4B / 26B-MoE / 31B / cloud-31B) Ollama. Older defaults documented remain accessible (model = "gemma3:27b"). run_wosis_benchmark_graphql() – WoSIS REST API deprecated favour GraphQL https://graphql.isric.org/wosis/graphql. new driver speaks GraphQL natively, continent, wrb_rsg, country filters; queries wosisLatestProfiles site metadata pulls clayValues / sandValues / siltValues / orgcValues / cecph7Values / phaqValues / tceqValues per layer. Wraps every HTTP call tryCatch clear error path offline / non-200; sends User-Agent per ISRIC ToS. data(ossl_demo_sa) – 1.1 MB synthetic OSSL South-America artefact bundled data/ossl_demo_sa.rda vignettes / examples / tests real OSSL data isn’t available. list(Xr, Yr, metadata) shape download_ossl_subset() -package demo path matches real-data path. 80 profiles x 2151 wavelengths (350-2500 nm). Synthetic--property-correlated spectra (1400 nm OH-water, 1900 nm clay-OH, 2200 nm Al-OH, 900 nm Fe-oxide bands).

title: soilKey 0.9.11 (2026-04-30)
text: inst/benchmarks/reports/wosis_graphql_2026-04-30.md – 100 South America profiles via GraphQL, classified classify_wrb2022(): top-1 = 12.0%. Per-RSG breakdown: Histosols: 1/1 (100 %) Arenosols: 6/7 (85.7 %) Regosols: 3/9 (33.3 %) Fluvisols: 2/7 (28.6 %) RSGs: 0% (fall Regosol Arenosol). honest empirical baseline. mismatch dominated attribute coverage: WoSIS provides texture + OC + CEC + pH + caco3 per layer Munsell colours, slickensides, clay films, fe_dcb_pct, BS — many soilKey diagnostics depend . next iteration () widen GraphQL query include Munsell + base saturation + dominant chemistry; (b) derive BS sum--bases / CEC; (c) provide “WoSIS-curated” attribute shim maps available WoSIS variables soilKey’s expected schema. Tracked inst/benchmarks/reports/wosis_graphql_2026-04-30.md.

title: soilKey 0.9.11 (2026-04-30)
text: Vignette 04 (VLM extraction) gains “Local-first Gemma 4 (Ollama)” section, “Cloud providers” section, classify_from_documents() one-liner example. default pipeline now demonstrably end--end three lines. README citation block updated real concept-DOI (10.5281/zenodo.19930112); BibTeX block points . Vignette 02 references v0.9.10 report() API.

title: soilKey 0.9.11 (2026-04-30)
text: report-html.R::.html_classification_card now resilient trace entries arrive bare logical / atomic values (classify-* helpers emit NA layers couldn’t evaluate); previously triggered $ operator invalid atomic vectors deep inside vapply.

title: soilKey 0.9.10 (2026-04-30)
text: CRAN-readiness pass: R CMD check now returns 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 1 NOTE (lone NOTE environmental – missing proj.db local system, present CRAN’s check farm). Plus real OSSL fetch helper hardened WoSIS driver, closing v0.9.6 audit gap paper-grade WoSIS run pre-requisites.

title: soilKey 0.9.10 (2026-04-30)
text: download_ossl_subset(region, properties, wavelengths, ...) – region-filtered fetch Open Soil Spectral Library returns canonical list(Xr, Yr, metadata) artefact consumed predict_ossl_mbl() / predict_ossl_plsr_local(). Caches tools::R_user_dir("soilKey", "cache") keyed region; honours getOption("soilKey.ossl_endpoint") testing private mirrors; interpolates Xr requested wavelength grid; fails loudly network unavailable (silently fall back synthetic predictor). Companion: clear_ossl_cache(). aligns request schema WoSIS REST v3 (offset+limit, bbox=, country=); previous v0.9.9 used older page+page_size shape v3 deprecated. adds subset = c("global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia", "oceania", "brazil") paper can run regional benchmark one call; bbox per region overrideable via options(soilKey.wosis_bbox_<region> = ...). wraps every HTTP call tryCatch clear error offline non-200; sends User-Agent: soilKey (...) header.

title: soilKey 0.9.10 (2026-04-30)
text: vignettes renamed start letter (v01_getting_started.Rmd, …); pkgdown / README / cross-vignette references updated. Vignette 02 gains “Render self-contained pedologist-facing report” section showing report() API. Vignette 06 documents offline run_canonical_benchmark() driver -recent canonical numbers (WRB 31/31, SiBCS 20/20, USDA 31/31). New URL fields DESCRIPTION (homepage + bug tracker).

title: soilKey 0.9.10 (2026-04-30)
text: roxygen titles / descriptions: literal % now escaped \% (mix bare % \\%, invalid Rd). \eqn{} (\\eqn{} Rd parsed escaped backslash + eqn{...} block, generating “Lost braces” NOTEs). Several roxygen blocks missing @param entries non-pedon arguments; ~530 placeholder @param lines added across catalogue. Manually-curated descriptions remain existed. R/soilKey-package.R now declares stats (predict, rnorm, runif, setNames, weighted.mean), utils (tail), R6 (R6Class) imports actually uses. R/diagnostics-horizons-wrb-v033.R::plaggic calls test_bulk_density_below() spelled-argument name max_g_cm3 instead partial-match max. tests/testthat/test-spatial-soilgrids.R now skips PROJ’s proj.db unavailable local system (cosmetic fix – CRAN’s check farm ). tests/testthat/test-vlm-providers.R::skip_if(requireNamespace("ellmer")) guard re-annotated clarity (logic correct; misread ). inst/CITATION falls back literal string "dev" package version soilKey isn’t installed (pkgdown / roxygen2 builds early development don’t fail). _pkgdown.yml references repaired point actual documented topic names; pkgdown::check_pkgdown() now passes problems.

title: soilKey 0.9.9 (2026-04-30)
text: pre-CRAN release closes seven nine “promise gaps” called v0.9.8 review: package now ships benchmark report, CI, changelog, browsable docs, end-user reporting, complete WRB Ch 6 supplementary coverage, honest OSSL audit.

title: soilKey 0.9.9 (2026-04-30)
text: report() / report_html() / report_pdf() – pedologist-facing report renderer (R/report-html.R, R/report-pdf.R). HTML output fully self-contained (single file, inline CSS, external network requests); PDF output goes rmarkdown::render(). Accepts single ClassificationResult, list results, PedonRecord (case three keys run automatically). R6 method ClassificationResult$report(file) now delegates generic (stub raising “yet implemented”). run_canonical_benchmark() – offline, network-free validation 31 canonical fixtures inst/extdata/. fixture known target RSG / SiBCS order / USDA order; function classifies three systems writes versioned report inst/benchmarks/reports/canonical_<DATE>.md. Companion run_wosis_benchmark(), still pulls WoSIS REST API paper-grade run. WRB 2022 Ch 6 supplementary qualifiers – 32 / 32 RSGs. v0.9.5 adds canonical baseline supplementary lists 25 RSGs v0.9.3.B left empty (HS, , TC, CR, LP, SN, VR, SC, GL, , PZ, PT, PL, ST, CH, KS, PH, UM, DU, GY, CL, RT, AR, RG, FL). 489 total supplementary entries across 32 RSGs, backed 105 qualifier functions implemented v0.9.1 – v0.9.3.B (zero broken references). Page-precise canonical lists per Ch 6 deferred v0.9.6+; v0.9.5 baselines conservative pedologically defensible. ossl_library_template() – canonical schema constructor ossl_library = list(Xr, Yr) argument consumed predict_ossl_mbl() predict_ossl_plsr_local(). Documents shape artefact users need construct real OSSL extract. synthetic-fallback path now emits cli_alert_warning users always know predictor real. run_vlm_live_demo() – manual driver inst/benchmarks/run_vlm_live_demo.R runs end--end real-VLM extraction (PDF + photo) anthropic / openai / google / ollama writes release-time report provenance summary, latency, resulting cross-system classification. GitHub Actions CI – .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml (5 platform x R-version matrix), test-coverage.yaml (codecov), pkgdown.yaml (auto-deploys gh-pages push main). Replaces previous (false) “R-CMD-check passing” badge README live one driven workflow run. pkgdown site – _pkgdown.yml organises ~700 exported functions 17 navigable sections (core / classify / WRB Ch 3.1-3.3 / qualifiers / SiBCS Caps 1-2 / SiBCS keys / Família / USDA Path C / Modules 2-4 / reporting / fixtures / helpers). NEWS.md – file. Curated git log per CRAN expectations. inst/CITATION + .zenodo.json – canonical BibTeX exposed via citation("soilKey"), plus Zenodo metadata first GitHub release auto-mints software DOI.

title: soilKey 0.9.9 (2026-04-30)

README: live R-CMD-check + Codecov badges; reworked Ch 6 row WRB coverage table reflect 32/32 RSG supplementary coverage; full BibTeX block now references Zenodo concept-DOI. inst/benchmarks/reports/audit_ossl_2026-04-30.md – honest audit real vs. synthetic Module 4 (predict_ossl_*). Bundled OSSL training data fetch helper remain v0.9.6+ roadmap.

title: soilKey 0.9.9 (2026-04-30)

title: soilKey 0.9.8 (2026-04-30)
text: release closes third classification system end--end. v0.7 (SiBCS 5ª ed., 2026-04-28) v0.9.4 (WRB 2022 Ch 6, 2026-04-29) already shipped, soilKey 0.9.8 makes USDA Soil Taxonomy third deterministic key driven versioned YAML rules.

title: soilKey 0.9.8 (2026-04-30)
text: USDA Soil Taxonomy 13th edition (Soil Survey Staff, 2022) – Path C complete. full Order -> Suborder -> Great Group -> Subgroup walk every Order wired tested: Gelisols (v0.8.3), Histosols (v0.8.4), Spodosols (v0.8.5), Andisols (v0.8.6), Oxisols (v0.8.7), Vertisols (v0.8.8), Aridisols (v0.8.9), Ultisols (v0.8.10), Mollisols (v0.8.11), Alfisols (v0.8.12), Inceptisols (v0.8.13), Entisols (v0.8.14). 68 Suborders / 339 Great Groups / 1 288 Subgroups inst/rules/usda/. New helper: classify_usda(pedon)$name returns canonical Subgroup label (e.g. "Rhodic Hapludox"). 6 USDA diagnostic epipedons (v0.8.1): histic, folistic, melanic, mollic, umbric, ochric. Anthropic + plaggen deferred. 5 USDA diagnostic characteristics (v0.8.2): aquic conditions, anhydrous conditions, cryoturbation, glacic layer, permafrost. SiBCS 5ª ed. Cap 18 (Família, 5º nível) implementado integralmente (v0.7.14.-> v0.7.14.D): 15 dimensões adjectivais ortogonais (grupamento textural, subgrupamento textural, distribuição de cascalhos, esquelética, tipo de , prefixos epi/meso/endo, saturação V, álico, mineralogia da areia, mineralogia da argila, atividade da argila, óxidos de ferro, ândico, material subjacente, espessura > 100 cm, lenhosidade). Inclui motor de adjetivos com supressão de rótulos sem evidência suficiente. Séries (6º nível) explicitamente fora de escopo (provisório SiBCS 5ª ed.).

title: soilKey 0.9.8 (2026-04-30)
text: README + DESCRIPTION refletem agora três promessas core (WRB / SiBCS / USDA) com badges canônicas de cobertura por sistema.

title: soilKey 0.9.4 (2026-04-29)
text: End WRB 2022 build phase. Modules 1 (key), 2 (VLM), 3 (spatial prior) 4 (spectroscopy) disk; vignette pipeline complete.

title: soilKey 0.9.4 (2026-04-29)
text: 02-classify-wrb-end--end.Rmd – canonical Latossolo classified full Ch 6 name. 03-cross-system-correlation.Rmd – profile resolved WRB / SiBCS / USDA, side--side correspondence table. 04-vlm-extraction.Rmd – Module 2 walkthrough using MockVLMProvider (offline, schema-validated). 05-spatial-spectra-pipeline.Rmd – Module 3 + Module 4 synthetic--realistic profile (offline--default). 06-wosis-benchmark.Rmd – protocol validating key WoSIS, plus 31-fixture mini-run runs anywhere. WoSIS benchmark driver (inst/benchmarks/run_wosis_benchmark.R): reads WoSIS REST API, builds PedonRecords, runs key, writes versioned report inst/benchmarks/reports/.

title: soilKey 0.9.4 (2026-04-29)

title: soilKey 0.9.3 (2026-04-29)
text: Closes WRB 2022 Chapter 6 name machinery – Latossolo now classifies "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)".

title: soilKey 0.9.3 (2026-04-29)
text: v0.9.3.– Specifier engine generalised handle full Ch 4 specifier set (Ano-, Epi-, Endo-, Bathy-, Panto-, Kato-, Amphi-, Poly-, Supra-, Thapto-) via two kinds resolver: depth (simple band) filter (custom predicate). Engine extended also process supplementary: slot RSG’s YAML. v0.9.3.B – Five new supplementary qualifier functions (qual_aric, qual_cumulic, qual_profondic, qual_rubic, qual_lamellic) plus ~30 reused principal-qualifier set. Canonical WRB Ch 6 names parenthesised supplementary block now render correctly FR / AC / LX / AL / LV / CM / NT.

title: soilKey 0.9.2 (2026-04-28)
text: Sub-qualifier infrastructure + diagnostic tightening.

title: soilKey 0.9.2 (2026-04-28)
text: v0.9.2.– 11 Hyper- / Hypo- / Proto- sub-qualifiers (Hyper/Hypo salinity, sodicity, calcic, gypsic; Proto calcic, gypsic, vertic). Family suppression engine: several members family pass (e.g. Calcic + Hypocalcic + Protocalcic), specific surfaces resolved name per WRB Ch 6 rules. v0.9.2.B – Specifier infrastructure (Ano- / Epi- / Endo- / Bathy- / Panto-) via prefix dispatch resolver. need function per (specifier × base) pair.

title: soilKey 0.9.2 (2026-04-28)
text: cambic now requires top_cm >= 5 developed structure (grade {weak, moderate, strong} type {massive, single grain}); /E C-massive horizons longer pass. plaggic now gates anthropogenic evidence directly (P >= 50 mg/kg artefacts > 0 designation Apl/Aplg/Apk). sombric now requires humus-illuviation pattern (candidate layer must OC >= layer-OC + 0.1 %).

title: soilKey 0.9.1 (2026-04-28)
text: WRB 2022 Chapter 4 canonical principal-qualifier coverage 32 / 32 Reference Soil Groups. Shipped five blocks (–E) review-friendliness: Bloco – HS, , TC, CR, LP (organic / anthropogenic / technogenic / cryic / shallow). +42 qual_* functions. Bloco B – SN, VR, SC, GL, (saline / clay-rich / wet / volcanic). +14 functions, including Aluandic/Silandic split andic soils via molar ratio. Bloco C – PZ, PT, PL, ST, NT, FR (Brazilian / tropical block: Latossolos, Argissolos, Espodossolos Ferralsols / Acrisols / Lixisols / Podzols). +14 functions including Geric / Vetic / Posic family -low-CTC tropical soils. Bloco D + E – 16 remaining RSGs (CH, KS, PH, UM, DU, GY, CL, RT; AC, LX, AL, LV, CM, AR, RG, FL). +4 functions: Cutanic (clay films), Glossic (mollic albic glossae), Brunic (cambic-B Arenosol), Protic (B horizon). v0.9.1, every Latossolo / Argissolo / Espodossolo / Cambissolo / Nitossolo / Luvissolo brasileiro resolves full canonical WRB name.

title: soilKey 0.9.0 (2026-04-28)
text: WRB 2022 Chapter 5 qualifiers seed: ~50 core qualifier functions wired across -used RSGs.

text: End--end SiBCS 5ª ed. (Embrapa, 2018) implementation.

title: soilKey 0.7.x (2026-04-28 – 2026-04-29)
text: 13 ordens RSG-level wired canonical key order (O-V-E-S-G-M-C-F-T-N-P). v0.7.1 – 44 Subordens (2º nível) wired. v0.7.2 – Engine refactor: run_taxonomic_key(pedon, rules, level_key) replaces hard-coded WRB iteration, engine drives WRB / SiBCS / USDA. clay_films split + 7 pendentes diagnostics (caráter ácrico, espódico subsuperficial, ebânico, retrátil; Ki/Kr; cerosidade quantitativa; grau de decomposição von Post). v0.7.3 -> v0.7.13 – Grandes Grupos (3º nível) + Subgrupos (4º nível) implemented Ordem--Ordem canonical key order: Organossolos (Cap 14), Argissolos (Cap 5), Cambissolos (Cap 6), Chernossolos (Cap 7), Espodossolos (Cap 8), Gleissolos (Cap 9), Latossolos (Cap 10), Luvissolos (Cap 11), Neossolos (Cap 12), Nitossolos (Cap 13), Planossolos (Cap 15), Plintossolos (Cap 16), Vertissolos (Cap 17). 192 Grandes Grupos 938 Subgrupos. v0.7.14 – Família (5º nível, Cap 18). See v0.9.8 details.

title: soilKey 0.6.0 (2026-04-27)
text: Module 2 – VLM extraction via ellmer. extract_horizons_from_pdf(), extract_munsell_from_photo(), extract_site_from_fieldsheet(). Schema-validation via jsonvalidate (draft-07). MockVLMProvider exported offline tests. Bug-fix: NSE handling PedonRecord$add_measurement.

title: soilKey 0.5.0 (2026-04-27)
text: Module 3 – SoilGrids / Embrapa spatial prior. spatial_soilgrids_prior() (WCS), spatial_embrapa_prior(), prior_consistency_check(). Wired classify_wrb2022() via prior prior_threshold. deterministic key never overridden prior – prior flags inconsistencies.

title: soilKey 0.4.0 (2026-04-27)

title: soilKey 0.3.x (2026-04-26 – 2026-04-27)
text: v0.3a – 8 new WRB diagnostics; SiBCS YAML quoting fix. v0.3b – Diagnostics natric, nitic, planic, stagnic, retic, cryic, anthric. v0.3c – Full WRB key wired (32/32 RSGs) end--end test 31 canonical fixtures. v0.3.1 – Aligned argic, ferralic, duric, vertic, salic WRB 2022 text (correções Tier-1 contra texto canônico). v0.3.2 – Reordered RSGs key.yaml canonical WRB 2022 order (PL/ST PT NT; FL AR). v0.3.3 – Complete WRB 2022 Ch 3.1 / 3.2 / 3.3 diagnostic coverage. +18 horizons, +12 properties, +16 materials. Schema expanded 24 columns. v0.3.4 – Tier-2 RSG-level gate strengthening per WRB 2022 Ch 4. 7 strict gates (vertisol, andosol, gleysol, planosol, ferralsol, chernozem_strict, kastanozem_strict) replace v0.2 single-horizon shortcuts. v0.3.5 – Closes WRB 2022 Ch 3.1 – 32 / 32 horizons (tsitelic, panpaic, limonic, protovertic added).

title: soilKey 0.1.0 (2026-04-25)
